reference: I te mutunga iho, me kaua tātou e ohorere mēnā i whakaea ō tātou ake tūpuna i tā rātou "raru pūmua" āhua pērā i tā ngā makimaki mōhio ki te whenua hawana (savanna) i tēnei wā.

source: Sleep interruption is the process of purposefully awakening during your normal sleep period and falling asleep a short time later (10–60 minutes).
reference: Ko te kokoti moe ko te hātepe o te āta whakaoho i te wā noa o tō moe, me te hoki ki te moe tētahi wā poto whai muri ake (10-60 ngā meneti).

source: This can be easily done by using a relatively quiet alarm clock to bring you to consciousness without fully waking you.
reference: Ka māmā te mahi pēnei mā te whakamahi i tētahi matawā whakaara āhua ngū kia tō mai koe ki te ao mārama karekau te oho katoa.

source: If you find yourself resetting the clock in your sleep, it can be placed on the other side of the room, forcing you to get out of bed to turn it off.
reference: Ki te kite koe i tō whakarite anō i te karaka i te wā e moe ana koe, ka taea te whakatakoto ki tērā atu taha o te rūma, hei reira koe me peke atu i te moenga hei weto.

source: Other biorhythm-based options involve drinking lots of fluid (particularly water or tea, a known diuretic) prior to sleep, forcing one to get up to urinate.
reference: Ētahi kē atu kōwhiringa o te mahi i runga i te rere o te rā ko te inu i te nui o ngā wē (inarā te wai, te tī rānei, pai mō te whakaputa mimi) i mua i te moe, nō reira me maranga te tangata ki te mimi.

source: The amount of inner peace a person possesses correlates oppositely to the amount of tension in one’s body and spirit.
reference: E hāngai tauaro ana te rahi o te rongo ā-roto o te tangata ki te rahi o te tanuku ki te tinana, te manawa pā ki te wairua hoki.

source: The lower the tension, the more positive the life force present. Every person has the potential to find absolute peace and contentment.
reference: Nā te iti ake o te mauri ka nui ake ai te mauri pai o koneki. Kei ia tangata te pito mata kia rapu i te rangimarie me te māuruuru roa.

source: Everyone can achieve enlightenment. The only thing standing in the way of this goal is our own tension and negativity.
reference: Ka taea e te katoa te tae atu ki te māramatanga. Ko te ārai anake e tū ana ki te ara o tēnei whāinga ko tō tātou ake manawa pā, whakakāhoretanga hoki.

source: The Tibetan Buddhism is based on the teachings of Buddha, but were extended by the mahayana path of love and by a lot of techniques from Indian Yoga.
reference: E hāngai ana te Tibetan Buddhism ki ā Buddha whakaakoranga, engari i whakawhānuingia e te ara aroha o mahayana me ngā tini āhua ā-mahi o te ioka nō Īnia.

source: In principle the Tibetan Buddhism is very simple. It consists of Kundalini Yoga, meditation and the path of all-embracing love.
reference: E mārama ana te Tibetan Buddhism. Ko te Kundalini Yoga, ko te whakamanatu me te huarahi mātua ki te aroha mutunga-kore.

source: With Kundalini Yoga the Kundalini energy (enlightenment energy) is awakened through yoga postures, breathing exercises, mantras and visualizations.
reference: Ki te Kundalini Yoga ka whakaohotia te ngao Kundalini (te ngao māramatanga) mā ngā tūnga ioka, ngā mahi hā, ngā kīanga whakatau, me te aronga ki ngā wawata.

source: The center of Tibetan meditation is the Deity Yoga. Through the visualization of various deities the energy channels are cleaned, the chakras are activated and the enlightenment consciousness is created.
reference: Ko te pokapū o te whakatau whakaaro Tipete ko te Deity Yoga. Mā te whakaaro ā-ataata ki ngā tini atua ka whakawāteatia ngā ia ngoi, ka kāngia ngā hakara (chakra), ā, ka hangaia te māramatanga.

source: Germany was a common enemy in World War 2, leading to cooperation between the USSR and USA. With the end of the war the clashes of system, process and culture led to the countries falling out.
reference: He hoariri whānui a Tiamani i te Pakanga Tuarua o te Ao, i runga i tērā ka mahi tahi a USSR me Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika. I te mutunga o te pakanga ka pukuriri ēnei whenua ki a rāua anō i te mea o te tūtukitanga o te pūnaha, o te hātepe me te ahurea i pēnei ai.

source: With two years of the end of the war, the former allies were now enemies and the Cold War began.
reference: I te rua tau o te mutunga o te pakanga, he hoariri ngā hoa o mua ināianei, ā, i tīmata te Cold War.

source: It was to last for the next 40 years and would be fought for real, by proxy armies, on battlefields from Africa to Asia, in Afghanistan, Cuba and many other places.
reference: Ka tū mō te 40 tau i te whai ake nei, ā, ka tūturu te whawhai, e ngā ope tauā whakakapi, ki ngā papariri atu i Āwherika ki Āhia, ki Afghanistan, ki Kīupa, ki ngā tini whenua anō.

source: By September 17, 1939, the Polish defense was already broken, and the only hope was to retreat and reorganise along the Romanian bridgehead.
reference: Ka tae ki te 17 o Hepetema, ka pakaru kē atu te pae ārai Polish, ko te tūmanako nui kia hoki anō, ka whakatika ake anō ki ngā whakawhitinga o Rōmania.

source: However, these plans were rendered obsolete nearly overnight, when over 800,000 soldiers from the Soviet's Union Red Army entered and created the Belarussian and Ukrainian fronts after invading the eastern regions of Poland in violation of the Riga Peace Treaty, the Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, and other international treaties, both bilateral and multilateral.
reference: Heoi anō, i noho korehāhā ēnei mahere i te tata ki te kotahi pō, i te kuhu atu me te hanga a te neke atu i te 800,000 hoia i te Red Army a te Soviet Union i ngā pae Belarussian me te Ukranian i muri i te eke ki ngā rohe rāwhiti o Pōrana me te takahi i te Riga Peace Treaty, te Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, me ētahi atu tiriti ā-ao, i waenganui i ngā whenua e rua, whenua takitini rānei.

source: Using ships to transport goods is by far the most efficient way to move large amounts of people and goods across oceans.
reference: Ko te whakamahinga o te kaipuke te rautaki pai rawa kia kawe i ngā tini tāngata me ngā rawa i te moana.

source: The job of navies has traditionally been to ensure that your country maintains the ability to move your people and goods, while at the same time, interfering with your enemy's ability to move his people and goods.
reference: Ko te mahi a te ope tauā moana he whakarite kia āhei ai tō whenua te āheinga kia neke i ō tāngata me ngā rawa, ā, i te wā orite e whakararu ana i ā te hoariri āheinga ki te neke i āna tāngata me āna rawa.

source: One of the most noteworthy recent examples of this was the North Atlantic campaign of WWII. The Americans were trying to move men and materials across the Atlantic Ocean to help Britain.
reference: Ko tētahi o ngā tauira whakamiramira nō nākuanei o tēnei ko te kauhanga riri North Atlantic o Te Pakanga Tuarua o te Ao. I ngana ngā Amerikana ki te whakaneke i ngā tāne, i ngā rawa whakawhiti atu i te Atlantic Ocean hei āwhina i a Peretānia.

source: At the same time, the German navy, using mainly U-boats, was trying to stop this traffic.
reference: I taua wā tonu, i te ngana te tauā-moana o Tiamani te whakatū i te hohoko mā te whakamahi i ngā poti-U.

source: Had the Allies failed, Germany probably would have been able to conquer Britain as it had the rest of Europe.
reference: Mena kua i hinga ngā hoawhawhai, Kua kore pea e riro i a Tiamana a Piritania pēnei i te rironga o te toinga o Europi.

source: Goats seem to have been first domesticated roughly 10,000 years ago in the Zagros Mountains of Iran.
reference: Ko te āhua nei i whakararatatia ngā nanekoti tata ki te 10,000 tau ki muri ki ngā Zagros Mountains ki Irāna.

source: Ancient cultures and tribes began to keep them for easy access to milk, hair, meat, and skins.
reference: Ka tīmata ngā ahurea me ngā iwi tawhito te pupuri i ērā kia māmā ai te āheinga atu ki te miraka, te huruhuru, te kiko, me ngā kirimoko.

source: Domestic goats were generally kept in herds that wandered on hills or other grazing areas, often tended by goatherds who were frequently children or adolescents, similar to the more widely known shepherd. These methods of herding are still used today.
reference: I rāhuitia ngā koti ki tētahi kāhui karore haere i ngā maunga me ngā pātiki, i manaakitia ai e te hunga tamariki, tōna rite ki te hēpara. E whakamahia tonutia ana ēnei tikanga ārahi kararehe i ēnei rā.

source: Wagonways were built in England as early as the 16th Century.
reference: I hangaia ngā kāta ki Ingaringi i te tīmatanga o te rautau 16.

source: Although wagonways merely consisted of parallel planks of wood, they allowed horses pulling them to achieve greater speeds and pull larger loads than on the slightly more rough roads of the day.
reference: Akakoa ko ngā ara wākena ko ngā papa rākau whakarara noa iho, i whakahei rātou i ngā hoiho e tō ana i a rātou te eke ki ngā tere nui ake me te tō i ngā wahanga nui ake i tērā ki ngā huarahi tuaranga o te wā.

source: Crossties were introduced fairly early to hold the tracks in place. Gradually, however, it was realised that tracks would be more efficient if they had a stip of iron on the top.
reference: I whakatō āhua moata ngā Crossties kia mau pū i ngā ara ki tana wāhi. Nāwai rā, heoi anō, i āta kitea i te painga kē atu o ngā ara mēnā i he wāhanga mētara iti ki runga.

source: This became common practice, but the iron caused more wear on the wooden wheels of the wagons.
reference: I tū tēnei hei tikanga noa, engari nā te rino i kaha ake te taiākotikoti ki ngā wīra wāpu o ngā wēkana.

source: Eventually, wooden wheels were replaced by iron wheels. In 1767, the first full-iron rails were introduced.
reference: Tāria te wā, i whakakapia ngā wīra rākau ki ngā wīra rino. I te tau 1767, i kitea tuatahitia ngā ara tereina tuatahi he rino-katoa.

source: The first known transportation was walking, humans began walking upright two million years ago with the emergence of Homo Erectus (meaning upright man).
reference: Ko te hīkoi te ikiiki tuatahi i mōhiotia, i tīmata tā te tangata hīkoi matika e rua miriona tau ki muri, i te aranga mai o te Homo Erectus (e kī ana te tangata matika).

source: Their predecessors, the Australopithecus did not walk upright as habitually.
reference: Ō rātou whakamuanga, ngā Australopithecus, kāore i pēnā rawa te hīkoi whakarunga i ngā wā katoa.

source: Bipedal specializations are found in Australopithecus fossils from 4.2-3.9 million years ago, although Sahelanthropus may have walked on two legs as early as seven million years ago.
reference: E kitea ana ngā āhuatanga rua-waewae i roto i ngā mātātoka Australopithecus mai i ngā tau 4.2-3.9 miriona ki muri, he ahakoa i hīkoi pea te Sahelanthropus ki ngā waewae e rua e whitu miriona tau ki muri.

source: We can start living more friendly to the environment, we can join to the environmental movement, and we can even be activists in order to reduce the future suffering in some degree.
reference: Ka taea e tātou te tīmata ki te noho pai ake me te taiao, ka taea e tātou te tūhono ki te kotahitanga mō te taiao, ka taea hoki e tātou te tū hei kaiwhakatūtū kia āhua whakahiki i te pākatokato hei te wā e heke mai ana.

source: This is just like symptomatic treatment in many cases. However, if we do not only want a temporary solution, then we should find the root of the problems, and we should deactivate them.
reference: E ōrite ana tēnei ki te whakamaimoatanga o ngā tohumate i ētahi wā. Heoi anō, ki te kore tātou e hiahia ki te rongoā taupua noa iho, me rapu tātou i te aka o ngā raru, ā, me whakakore e tātou.

source: It is obvious enough that the world has changed much because of humankind's scientific and technological advancements, and problems have become greater because of overpopulation and mankind's extravagant lifestyle.
reference: E mārama ana kua huri nei ao i ngā mahi a te ira tangata ā-pūtaiao nei, ā-hangarau nei hoki, ā, kua nui kē atu ngā raruraru kua pā mai i te kōpā rawa o te noho a ngā iwi i te ātaahuatanga a te noho a tatou te tangata.

source: After its adoption by Congress on July 4, a handwritten draft signed by the President of Congress John Hancock and the Secretary Charles Thomson was then sent a few blocks away to the printing shop of John Dunlap.
reference: Whai muri i tana whakaaetanga e te Congress i te rā 4 o Hūrae, ka tukuna tētahi tuhinga hukihuki kua hainatia e te President of Congress a John Hancock rāua ko Secretary Charles Thompson ētahi poraka atu ki te toa tā a John Dunlap.

source: Through the night between 150 and 200 copies were made, now known as "Dunlap broadsides".
reference: I roto i te pō i waenganui i te 150 me te 200 ngā kape i hangaia, e mōhiotia ana ināianei ko ngā "Dunlap broadsides".

source: The first public reading of the document was by John Nixon in the yard of Independence Hall on July 8.
reference: Nā John Nixon te pānuitanga tūmatanui tuatahi o te puka ki te iari o te Independence Hall i te 8 o Hūrae.

source: One was sent to George Washington on July 6, who had it read to his troops in New York on July 9. A copy reached London on August 10.
reference: I tukuna tētahi ki a George Washington i te 6 o Hūrae, ka pānuitia ki ōna hōia i New york i te 9 o Hūrae. I tae he kape ki Rānana i te 10 o Akuhata.

source: The 25 Dunlap broadsides still known to exist are the oldest surviving copies of the document. The original handwritten copy has not survived.
reference: Ko ngā Dunlap taha-whānui (Broadsides) e 25 e mōhiotia tonutia ana ko ngā tāruatanga tawhiti rawa e toe ana o te puka. Kāore te tuhinga tūturu ā-ringa i te toe tonu.

source: Many paleontologists today believe that one group of dinosaurs survived and is alive today. We call them birds.
reference: Tokomaha ngā tohunga mātātoka i tēnei wā e whakapono ana ko tētahi rōpū mokonui i mōrehurehu, ā, kei te ora tonu i tēnei wā. E kīia ana e tātou he manu.

source: Many people don't think about them as dinosaurs because they have feathers and can fly.
reference: He tini ngā tāngata kāore e whakapop ana he tainahoro ērā rā i te mea he huruhuru ōna, ā, e taea te rere.

source: But there are a lot of things about birds that still look like a dinosaur.
reference: He tini ngā mea o te manu e rite tonu nei ki te tainahora.

source: They have feet with scales and claws, they lay eggs, and they walk on their two back legs like a T-Rex.
reference: He waewae unaunahi me te matikuku, ka whakawhānau hēki, ā, ka hīkoi i runga i ōna waewae ō muri pēnei i te T-Rex.

source: Virtually all computers in use today are based on the manipulation of information which is coded in the form of binary numbers.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā ko ngā rorohiko e whakamahia nei i ēnei rā nei kua hanga anō he rāwekeweke i ngā pitopito kōrero kua hangaia i roto i ngā āhuatanga o ngā nama tāhūrua.

source: A binary number can have only one of two values, i.e. 0 or 1, and these numbers are referred to as binary digits - or bits, to use computer jargon.
reference: Ka taea anake e tētahi tau tāhūrua te whai i tētahi o ngā uara e rua, arā te 0, te 1 rānei, ā, e kīia ana ēnei tau hei mati tāhūrua - hei moka ranei, kia whakamahia te kupu rorohiko.

source: Internal poisoning may not be immediately apparent. Symptoms, such as vomiting are sufficiently general that an immediate diagnosis cannot be made.
reference: Kāore pea te paihana ō roto e kitea inamatangia. Ko ngā tohu mate, pēnei i te ruaki he noa te āhua kia kore e taea te whakatau māuiui i taua wā tonu.

source: The best indication of internal poisoning may be the presence of an open container of medication or toxic household chemicals.
reference: Ko te tohu pai rawa o te paihana ā-roto ko te noho ki reira a tētahi ipu tuwhera o te rongoā, o ngā matū ā-whare tāoke rānei.

source: Check the label for specific first aid instructions for that specific poison.
reference: Tirohia te tapanga mō ngā tohutohu āwhina tuatahi mō taua paihana motuhake.

source: The term bug is used by entomologists in a formal sense for this group of insects.
reference: Ko te kupu ngārara, e ai ki ngā tohunga ngārara mō tēnei momo horopaki, he pēpeke.

source: This term derives from ancient familiarity with Bed-bugs, which are insects highly adapted to parasitize humans.
reference: Kei te ahu mai tēnei kupu i te waiatanga tahito ki ngā Bed-bugs, arā, he pepeke kua tino urutau ki te noho pirinoa ki te tangata.

source: Both Assassin-bugs and Bed-bugs are nidicolous, adapted to living in nest or housing of their host.
reference: Ka whānau pangore mai ngā Assassin-bugs me ngā Bed-bugs, kua whanaketia kia noho ki te kōhanga, ki te kāinga rānei o tō rātou rauropi papa.

source: Across the United States of America, there are approximately 400,000 known cases of Multiple Sclerosis (MS), leaving it as the leading neurological disease in younger and middle aged adults.
reference: Puta noa i Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika he tata ki te 400,000 kēhi e mōhiotia ana o te Multiple Sclerosis (MS), nō reira ko ia te tahumaero ā-roro matua ki ngā pakeke he rangatahi, he waenga rānei te pakeke.

source: MS is a disease that affects the central nervous system, which is made up of the brain, the spinal cord and the optic nerve.
reference: Ko MS tētahi tahumaero e pā ana ki te pūnaha ioio matua, e hangaia ana ki te roro, te aho tuaiwi me te ioio mai i te roro ki te karu.

source: Research has found that females are two times more likely to have MS then males.
reference: Kua kitea e te rangahau ko te tupono kia pāngia te wahine e te MS e rua whakareatanga ki tā te tāne.

source: A couple may decide it is not in their best interest, or in the interest of their child, to raise a baby.
reference: Ka whakatau pea tētahi tokorua kāore i te whai tikanga pai rawa mō rāua, mō tā rāua tamaiti rānei, te whakatipu pēpi.

source: These couples may choose to make an adoption plan for their baby.
reference: Ka kōwhiri pea ēnei tokorua te hanga i tētahi mahere whāngai mō tā rāua pēpi.

source: In an adoption, the birth parents terminate their parental rights so that another couple may parent the child.
reference: Ki te whāngai, ka motu ngā mātua whakawhānau i ō rātou motika ā-mātua kia whakatipuria te tamaiti e tētahi atu tokorua.

source: Science’s main goal is to figure out the way the world works through the scientific method. This method in fact guides most scientific research.
reference: Ko te whāinga matua a te pūtaiao he mōhio ki te mahinga o te ao mā te tikanga pūtaiao. Ko tēnei tikanga nei hei arataki i te nuinga o ngā rangahau pūtaiao.

source: It isn’t alone though, experimentation, and an experiment is a test that is used to eliminate one or more of the possible hypotheses, asking questions, and making observations also guide scientific research.
reference: Ehara i te mea ko ia ki tōna kotahi, te mātaitanga, ā, ko te mātaitanga ko tētahi whakamātautau e whakamahia ana hei whakakore i te whakapae kotahi, neke atu rānei ka taea, te ui i ngā pātai, me te whakatātare hei ārahi hoki i ngā rangahau pūtaiao.

source: Naturalists and philosophers focused on classical texts and, in particular, on the Bible in Latin.
reference: I aro te hunga aro taiao me ngā wānanga ki ngā pukapuka o nehe, inarā, ki te paipera ki te reo Rātini.

source: Accepted were Aristotle's views on all matters of science, including psychology.
reference: I whakaaetia ngā tirohanga o Aristotle ki ngā kaupapa katoa o te pūtaiao, tae atu ki te mātai hinengaro.

source: As knowledge of Greek declined, the West found itself cut off from its Greek philosophical and scientific roots.
reference: I te heke haere o te reo Kariki, ka haukotia te Uru i ōna pakiaka wānanga, pūtaiao Kariki hoki.

source: Many observed rhythms in physiology and behavior often crucially depend on the presence of endogenous cycles and their production through biological clocks.
reference: He rahi tonu ngā tāngata i mātai i te manawataki o te koiora, e mārama ai ki ngā whanonga me tirohia ngā huringa o te tinana me ngā hua ka puta i ngā mahi a te tinana.

source: Periodic rhythms, which are not simply responses to external periodic cues, have been documented for most living beings, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.
reference: Ko ngā ia ā-wā, arā, ehara ēnei i ngā urupare noa iho ki ngā tohu ā-wā ki waho, kua kitea mō te tata ki te katoa o ngā koiora, tae atu ki ngā huakita, ngā hekaheka, ngā tipu, ngā kararehe anō hoki.

source: Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.
reference: Ko ngā karaka koiora he mea ngapu kei te tautīnei i a rātou anō ka haere tonu te wā o te huringa rere noa ahakoa kei te ngaro ngā tohu nō waho.

source: The Hershey and Chase experiment was one of the leading suggestions that DNA was a genetic material.
reference: Ko te whakamātautau Hershey me Chase tētahi o ngā whakaaro i whakatau ai he mea ira te pītau ira.

source: Hershey and Chase used phages, or viruses, to implant their own DNA into a bacterium.
reference: I whakamahingia e Hershey me Chase ngā wāhanga, ngā huaketo rānei hei whakauru i ō rātou ake pītau ira ki roto ki tētahi kitakita.

source: They did two experiments marking either the DNA in the phage with a radioactive phosphorus or the protein of the phage with radioactive sulfur.
reference: I whakamahia e rātau ētahi whakamātautau e rua e tohu ana i te pītau ira ki te huaketo he phage te momo me tētahi hīnātore iraruke, ki te pūmua o te huaketo phage me te pūngāwhā iraruke rānei.

source: Mutations can have a variety of different effects depending on the type of mutation, the significance of the piece of genetic material affected and whether the cells affected are germ-line cells.
reference: He maha ngā momo iranoi, kei runga i tōna momo e kitea ai ka pēhea e raru ai, ko te hihiri o te momo ira kua pāngia, ā, mēnā rānei he momo meroiti ngā ira e pāngia ana.

source: Only mutations in germ-line cells can be passed on to children, while mutations elsewhere can cause cell-death or cancer.
reference: Ko ngā rerekētanga anake ki ngā raina-ira ka tukuna ki te hunga tamariki, ko ētahi atu rā ka hua mai te mate-ira, te mate pukupuku rānei.

source: Nature-based tourism attracts people interested in visiting natural areas for the purpose of enjoying the scenery, including plant and animal wildlife.
reference: Kei te whakamanea te tāpoitanga ki te taiao i ngā tāngata e manawa reka ana te toro atu ki ngā wāhi māori mō te ngahau i te tirohanga te take, tae atu ki ngā tipu, ki ngā kararehe.

source: Examples of on-site activities include hunting, fishing, photography, bird watching, and visiting parks and studying information about the ecosystem.
reference: Hei whakatauira ake i ngā mahi ki konei, ko te aruaru, ko te hī ika, ko ngā mahi tango whakaahua, ko te mātai manu, ko te tirotiro whenua me te ako i ngā mōhiotanga e pā ana ki te taiao.

source: An example is visiting, photographing, and learning about organgatuangs in Borneo.
reference: Ko tētahi tauira ko te toro, te mau whakaahua me te ako e pā ana ki ngā orangitangi ki Borneo.

source: Every morning, people leave small country towns in cars to go their workplace and are passed by others whose work destination is the place they have just left.
reference: Ia ata, ka wehewehe ngā tāngata i ngā tāone iti ki taiwhenua ki ngā waka ki te haere ki tō rātou wāhi mahi, ā, ka whakawhititia e ētahi atu ko tō rātou wāhi mahi ko te wāhi kātahi anō rātou ka wehe.

source: In this dynamic transport shuttle everyone is somehow connected with, and supporting, a transport system based on private cars.
reference: I roto i tēnei pahi kawekawe uekaha nei kua honotahi, kua tautoko hoki te katoa i tētahi pūnaha kawenga e hāngai ana ki ngā waka tūmatawhāiti.

source: Science now indicates that this massive carbon economy has dislodged the biosphere from one of its stable states that has supported human evolution for the past two million years.
reference: Ināianei, e tohu ana te pūtaiao ko te ohanga nui mō te waro kei te tūraki i te rangipūātea mai i tōna mauri tau, i manaakitia ai te ira tangata mō te 2 miriona tau ki muri.

source: Everyone participates in society and uses transportation systems. Almost everyone complains about transportation systems.
reference: Ka whai wāhi atu te kato ki ngā pūnaha mauranga. Ko te nuiga ka amuamu mō ngā pūnaha mauranga.

source: In developed countries you seldom hear similar levels of complaints about water quality or bridges falling down.
reference: I ngā whenua kua whanake, me uaua ka rongo koe i ngā momo amuamu mō te pai o te wai, mō te turakitanga rānei o ngā piriti.

source: Why do transportation systems engender such complaints, why do they fail on a daily basis? Are transportation engineers just incompetent? Or is something more fundamental going on?
reference: He aha ka puta mai ngā amuamu pērā i ngā pūnaha ikiiki, ā, he aha ka hinga i ia rā? He koretake noa ngā kaipūkaha ikiiki? He take pūtake ake kei te haere rānei?

source: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.
reference: Ko Traffic Flow te mātai i te rerenga o ngā kaitaraiwa takitahi me ngā waka i waenganui i ngā pūwāhi e rua me ngā pāhekoheko a tētahi ki tētahi.

source: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.
reference: E pōuri ana, he uaua te rangahau i te rere o ngā waka nā te mea e kore e taea te matapae i te whanonga taraiwa ki te mōhiotanga kotahi-rau ōrau.

source: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.
reference: Maringanui, kei roto i te whānui āhua ū ngā whanonga noa o ngā kaitaraiwa; nō reira, ka āhua ōrite ngā ia waka, ā, ka taea te whakaata āwhiwhi ā-pāngarau.

source: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.
reference: Kia pai ake te whakaatu i te rerenga waka, kua whakatūria ngā hononga i waenganui i ngā āhuatanga matua e toru: (1) te rere, (2) te apiapi, ā, (3) te tere.

source: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.
reference: He mea āwhina ēnei hononga ki te whakamahere, te hoahoa, me te mahi i ngā hanganga taha huarahi.

source: Insects were the first animals to take to the air. Their ability to fly helped them evade enemies more easily and find food and mates more efficiently.
reference: Ko ngārara ngā kararehe tuatahi i whai hā. Ko ō rātou pukenga ki te rere i māmā ake ai te ārai atu i ngā hoariri, ki te kai kai, ki te kimi hoa hoki.

source: Most insects have the advantage of being able to fold their wings back along the body.
reference: Kei te nuinga o ngā pepeke te huanga o te āheinga ki te kōnumi i ō rātou parirau ki te tinana.

source: This gives them a wider range of small places to hide from predators.
reference: Mā korā ka whānui ake ngā wāhi iti hei huna i ngā konihi.

source: Today, the only insects that cannot fold back their wings are dragon flies and mayflies.
reference: I tēnei rā, ko ngā pepeke e kore e taea te kōnumi i ō rātou parirau ko ngā uruururoroa, ko ngā piriwai anake.

source: Thousands of years ago, a man called Aristarchus said that the Solar System moved around the Sun.
reference: I ngā mano tau i mua, ko te tangata e kīa nei ko Aristarchus i kī ake ko te whānau a Tamanui te rā i huri rauna i te rā.

source: Some people thought he was right but many people believed the opposite; that the Solar System moved around the Earth, including the Sun (and even the other stars).
reference: I whakapono ētahi tāngata e tika ana ia engari ka rerekē te whakapono a ētahi; arā kia huri haere te Whānau a Tamanuiterā i te Ao, tae atu ki te Rā (tae rā anō ki ērā atu whetū).

source: This seems sensible, because the Earth doesn't feel as if it's moving, does it?
reference: He whaitake te āhua, nā te mea kāore i te rangona te neke haeretanga o te ao, nē?

source: The Amazon River is the second longest and the biggest river on Earth. It carries more than 8 times as much water as the second biggest river.
reference: Ko te awa Amazon te awa nunui, roroa tuarua o te ao. Neke atu i te 8 reanga wai e pūritia ana e ia.

source: The Amazon is also the widest river on Earth, at times six miles wide.
reference: Ko te Amazon te awa whānui o Papatūānuku nei, e ono maero te whānuitanga.

source: A full 20 percent of the water that pours out of the planet's rivers into the oceans comes from the Amazon.
reference: Kei te ahu mai te nui o te 20 ōrau o te wai e rere ana i ngā awa o te ao ki ngā moana i te Amazon.

source: The main Amazon River is 6,387 km (3,980 miles). It collects water from thousands of smaller rivers.
reference: Ko te roa o te Amazon River matua ko te 6,387 km (3,980 maero). Kei te kohi ia i te wai mai i ngā tini mano awa iti iho.

source: Although pyramid-building in stone continued until the end of the Old Kingdom, the pyramids of Giza were never surpassed in their size and the technical excellence of their construction.
reference: Ahakoa i haere tonu te hanga-koeko ki te kohatu tae atu ki te mutunga o te Old Kingdom, kore rawa i pahika ngā koeko o Giza i te rahi me te kounga hangarau o te hanganga.

source: New Kingdom ancient Egyptians marvelled at their predecessors monuments, which were then well over a thousand year old.
reference: I mīharo ngā tāngata Īhipa tawhito New Kingtom ki ngā tohu whakamaumahara o ō rātou tūpuna, i taua wā ka nui ake i te kotahi mano tau te tawhito.

source: Vatican City's population is around 800. It is the smallest independent country in the world and the country with the lowest population.
reference: Tata ki te 800 te taupori o Vatican City. Koirā te whenua motuhake iti rawa o te ao, waihoki ko te whenua he iti rawa te taupori.

source: Vatican City uses Italian in its legislation and official communications.
reference: Ko te reo ka whakamahia e te Whatikana ki roto i ōna ture me ōna pārongorongo ko te reo Itāria.

source: Italian is also the everyday language used by most of those who work in the state while Latin is often used in religious ceremonies.
reference: Ko te reo Itāria hoki te reo o ia rā e whakamahia ana e te nuinga o rātou e mahi ana ki te takiwā, ā, kei te tino whakamahia te reo Rātini ki ngā kawa ā-hāhi.

source: All citizens of Vatican City are Roman Catholic.
reference: Ko ngā kainoho katoa o Vatican City he Rōmana Katorika.

source: People have known about basic chemical elements such as gold, silver, and copper from antiquity, as these can all be discovered in nature in native form and are relatively simple to mine with primitive tools.
reference: Kua mōhio te tangata ki ngā pūmotu matū pūtake pērā i te koura, te hiriwa, te kapa mai rā anō, nā te mea ka taea te kite i te katoa o ēnei ki te taiao ki te āhuahanga tūturu, ā, he āhua māmā te maina ki ngā taputapu nō nehe.

source: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.
reference: Aristotle, te tangata whakaaro hohonu, i whakapae kua hanga ngā mea katoa i roto i ngā ranunga o tētahi o ngā pūmotu e whā. Ko te one, ko te wai, ko te hau me te ahi.

source: This was more like the four states of matter (in the same order): solid, liquid, gas, and plasma, though he also theorised that they change into new substances to form what we see.
reference: Ka pērā anō tēnei i ngā āhuatanga e whā o te matū (ki te raupapa ōrite): arā te totoka, te wē, te hau, te wētoto, ahakoa kua puta te ariā i a ia ka panoni ēnei ki ngā matū hou hei hanga i ngā mea e kitea ana e tātou.

source: Alloys are basically a mixture of two or more metals. Don't forget that there are many elements on the periodic table.
reference: Ko ngā koranu ko te ranunga noa o ngā mētara e rua, neke atu rānei. Kauaka e wareware he nui ngā pūmotu ki te taka pūmotu

source: Elements like calcium and potassium are considered metals. Of course, there are also metals like silver and gold.
reference: Ko ngā matū pērā i te konumohe me te konupūma e whai whakaarotia ana he mētara. Āe mārika, he mētara atu anō pēnei i te hiriwa me te koura.

source: You can also have alloys that include small amounts of non-metallic elements like carbon.
reference: Ka taea ngā kōranu e mau kōranu-kore ana mō ētahi mātū, pērā ki te waro.

source: Everything in the Universe is made of matter. All matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.
reference: Ngā mea katoa o te ao nei kua hangaia ki te matū. Ko ngā matū katoa kua hangaia ki ngā mea iti nei e kīa nei ko te ngota.

source: Atoms are so incredibly tiny that trillions of them could fit into the period at the end of this sentence.
reference: He pērā rawa te tino riki rawa o ngā ngota ka taea e ngā tini tiriona ngota te noho ki roto i te irakati ki te mutunga o tēnei rerenga.

source: Thus, the pencil was a good friend to many people when it came out.
reference: Nā tēnā, i whakahoahoa ai te penerakau ki te tini tangata i te wā i hangaia mai.

source: Sadly, as newer methods of writing have emerged, the pencil has been relegated to lesser status and uses.
reference: Te pōuri hoki, kua iti ake te mana me te whakamahinga o te pene rākau i te mea kua ara ake ētahi tikanga tuhituhi hou.

source: People now write messages on computer screens, never having to come close to a sharpener.
reference: Ka tuhia ngā karere ki runga rorohiko ināianei, hei aha noa iho te tāonga whakakoi

source: One can only wonder what the keyboard will become when something newer comes along.
reference: Ka taea anake te whai whakaaro ake ka ahatia ki te papa pātuhi inā ka puta mai tētahi mea hou.

source: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons.
reference: Ko te tikanga o te pahū fission he hari i te ngoi hei whakaemi i nukara he tini ngā iraoho me ngā iramoe.

source: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy.
reference: E takahurihuri ana pēnei i ngā kāta tumaha mā runga i te puke. E whakawehe nei anō i te poka pū, ka tukuna ai ētahi o taua ngoi.

source: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.
reference: He karihi pāhekeheke kei ētahi ngota, nā korā ka whakawehi ai ahakoa kāore he tuke, he iti rānei te tuke.

source: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust.
reference: Kua hangaia te mata o te Marama ki ngā kōhatu me te puehu. E kīia ana ko te papa ā-waho o te Marama ko te kirimarama.

source: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side.
reference: He tata ki te 70 km te mātotoru o te kirinuku ki te taha tata, ā, e 100 km te mātotoru ki te taha tawhiti.

source: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands.
reference: Ko raro i te maria he tūpuhi, ki tuawhenua he whānui ake.

source: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface.
reference: Ka nui ake pea te maria ki te taha tata i te mea he kōrahirahi ake te kirinuku. He māmā ake kia whanake ake te tahepuia ki te mata.

source: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them.
reference: Kei te ngako o ngā ariā kiko ko te rapu i ngā mea e tino whakaoho ana i te tangata, e pai ana ki a rātou rānei.

source: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood.
reference: Kei te marohi ēnei ariā kei ētahi tāngata he hiahia, he pīrangi hoki/rānei kua whakatōngia ki roto i a rātou e pakeke haere ana ki te pakeketanga.

source: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things.
reference: Kei te aro atu ēnei ariā ki ngā āhuatanga o ētahi tāngata e tuku ana i a rātou ki te hiahia i ngā mea e hiahiatia ana e rātou, ā, he aha ngā mea ki tō rātou taiao ka whakarite i a rātou ki te mahi, ki te kore mahi rānei i ētahi mahi.

source: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.
reference: Ko ngā ariā kiko rorotu e rua ko te Hierarchy of Needs Theory a Maslow, me te Two Factor Theory a Hertzberg.

source: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals).
reference: I te nuinga o te wā, e rua ngā whanonga ka puta mai pea i te tīmatanga o ngā kaiwhakahaere te ārahi i ō rātou hoamahi o mua. Ki tētahi pito ko te ngana ki te noho tonu hei "tētahi o ngā autaia" (ngā kō rānei).

source: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable.
reference: He uaua ki tēnei momo kaiwhakahaere te whakatau i ngā whakatau kāore e rataina, te whakamahi i ngā mahi whakatika, te arotake mahi, te tuku haepapa, me te whakarite kia noho haepapa ngā tāngata.

source: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own.
reference: Ki tērā atu pito o te whānuitanga, ka huri tētahi ki tētahi takitahi kāore e mōhiotia ana ki a ia me panoni ia i ngā mahi katoa kua mahia e te rōpū kia noho ko tōna ake.

source: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team.
reference: Waihoki, kei te noho haepapa te kaiārahi mō te eke me te hinga o te kapa.

source: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team.
reference: He nui ngā wā ka puta i tēnei whanonga ngā wehenga i waenganui i ngā kaiārahi me te toenga o te rōpū.

source: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences.
reference: E mau nei te taumata kōunga mō ngā roopū mariko ki ngā tīma taketake, heoi he rerekētanga iti nei.

source: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group.
reference: I ētahi wā ka noho ngā mema tīma mariko hei āhuatanga whakapākanga mō tō rātou rōpū ā-kiko tata.

source: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā he nui ake tō rātou tino rangatiratanga tēnā i ngā mema o ngā tīma noa, i te mea ko ā rātou tīma ka hui kei te āhua o ngā rohe wā rerekē ka kore pea e māramatia e tā rātou kaiwhakahaere ā-rohe.

source: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team.
reference: Ko te noho mai o tētahi "kapa kāore i te kitea" tūturu (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) hoki tētahi wāhanga ahurei o tētahi kapa mariko.

source: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member.
reference: Ko te "tīma kore kitea" te tīma whakahaere e noho ai hei rīpoatatanga mā ngā mema. Ka whakatū te tīma kore kitea ngā taumata mō ia mema.

source: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation.
reference: He aha i hiahia ai tētahi umanga te mahi i tētahi hātepe whakapau wā ki te whakarite i tētahi umanga whakaako? Ko tētahi whāinga mō te whakamahi i ngā kaupapa ako ki roto i tētahi parakatihi auaha.

source: When all available resources are effectively used across the functional departments of an organization, creativity and ingenuity can transpire.
reference: I te wā he pai te whakamahi i ngā rawa katoa e wātea ana whakawhiti atu i ngā tari mahi o tētahi whakahaere, ka ara ake pea te auaha me te atamai.

source: As a result, the process of an organization working together to overcome an obstacle can lead to a new innovative process to serve the customer's need.
reference: Me te aha, ko te hātepe o tētahi whakahaere e mahi tahi ana kia eke panuku i tētahi ārai e taea ai te arataki ki tētahi hātepe auaha hou hei whakarato i tā te kiritaki hiahia.

source: Before an organization can be innovative, leadership must create a culture of innovation as well as shared knowledge and organizational learning.
reference: E maromahue ai tētahi hinonga, me whakatōkia e ōna rangatira tētahi momo wairua maromahue, te tuari mātauranga, me tēnei mea te ako hoki.

source: Angel (2006), explains the Continuum approach as a method being used to help organizations reach a higher level of performance.
reference: E whakamārama ana a Angel (2006) i te huarahi Kore-otinga hei tikanga ārahi i ngā rōpū whakahaere kia eke ai rātou ki ngā taumata e whāia ana.

source: Neurobiological data provide physical evidence for a theoretical approach to the investigation of cognition. Therefore it narrows the research area and makes it much more exact.
reference: Kei ngā raraunga koiora-ā-io ngā whakaaturanga ā-kiko mō tētahi aronga ā-ariā ki te mātai i te whakaaroaro. Nā reira māna te kaupapa rangahau e whakawhāiti, ā, ka pū ake hoki te aronga.

source: The correlation between brain pathology and behaviour supports scientists in their research.
reference: Kei te tautoko te hāngaitanga i waenganui i te pānga hinengaro me te whanonga i ngā kaipūtaiao ki ā rātou rangahau.

source: It has been known for a long time that different types of brain damage, traumas, lesions, and tumours affect behaviour and cause changes in some mental functions.
reference: Kua roa e mōhiotia ana mā ngā momo rerekē o ā te roro mate, whara, panoni whanokē, pukupuku anō hoki e pā ki te whanonga, ā, ka hanga panoni hoki ki ētahi mahi ā-hinengaro.

source: The rise of new technologies allows us to see and investigate brain structures and processes never seen before.
reference: Ko te pikinga ake o ngā hangarau hou e tuku ana i a tātou ki te kite, ki te mātai i ngā hanganga me ngā hātepe roro kāore anō kia kitea.

source: This provides us with a lot of information and material to build simulation models which help us to understand processes in our mind.
reference: Ka homai tēnei i ngā tini pārongo me ngā rauemi hei hanga i ngā tauira whakaata e āwhina ai i a tātou kia whai māramatanga mō ngā hātepe o roto i ō tātou hinengaro.

source: Although AI has a strong connotation of science fiction, AI forms a very important branch of computer science, dealing with behavior, learning and intelligent adaptation in a machine.
reference: Ahakoa ko te AI e hāngai pū ana ki ngā pakiwaitara pūtaiao, he mea nui te AI ki te ao rorohiko, ko te whakamātau i ngā whanonga, ko te ako me te urutau i te taumata whakaaro ki tētahi mīhini.

source: Research in AI involves making machines to automate tasks that require intelligent behavior.
reference: Kei roto i te rangahau atamai horihori te hanga mīhini kia whakangāwari i ngā mahi e hiahia ana ki te whanonga atamai.

source: Examples include control, planning and scheduling, the ability to answer customer diagnoses and questions, as well as handwriting recognition, voice and face.
reference: Ko ētahi tauira ko te whakahaere, te whakamahere me te whakarite wā, te āheinga ki te whakahoki i ngā whakatau me ngā pātai a ngā kiritaki, tae atu hoki ki te mōhiotanga o te tuhi ā-ringa, te reo me te kanohi.

source: Such things have become separate disciplines, which focus on providing solutions to real life problems.
reference: Kua rerekē haere ēnei tūmomo āhuatanga, ko te aronga nui hoki he kimi rongoā mō nga mate tuturu nei.

source: The AI ​​system is now often used in the fields of economics, medicine, engineering and the military, as has been built in several home computer and video game software applications.
reference: Kei te whakamahia nuitia te pūnaha Atamai Horihori ki ngā mātaitanga o te ohaoha, te rongoā, te kaipūkaha, te tauā hoki, pērā i te hanganga ki ētahi taupānga pūmanawa rorohiko ā-kāinga, kēmu ataata hoki.

source: Field trips are a large part of any classroom. Quite often a teacher would love to take her students places to which a bus trip is not an option.
reference: He wāhanga nui te haerenga o te akomanga. I ētahi wā ka tino hiahia te kaiako kia haere ōna ākonga ki tōna taha ki ngā wāhi e kore e taea mā runga pahi.

source: Technology offers the solution with virtual field trips. Students can look at museum artifacts, visit an aquarium, or admire beautiful art while sitting with their class.
reference: Ko te huarahi a te ao matihiko ko te whakahaere haerenga ā-tuihono nei. Ka kitea e ngā tauira i ngā pātaka pupuri tāonga, te haere ki te tirotiro ki te puna moana, he whakanui ake rānei i te ātaahuatanga toi i ā rātou e noho ana ki te karaehe.

source: Sharing a field trip virtually is also a great way to reflect a on a trip and share experiences with future classes.
reference: He ara pai te haere tahi ā-mariko i tētahi haerenga hei whakaatu whakaaro mō te haerenga, me te kōrero mō ngā wheako ki ngā akomanga e haere ake nei.

source: For example, each year students from Bennet School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the State Capital, each year the website gets remodeled, but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.
reference: Hei tauira, ia tau ngā ākonga mai i te kura o Peneti i te raki o Karorina ka hanga i tētahi whārangi ipurangi mō tā rātou haerenga ki te Tāte Matua, ia tau ka whakahoungia te whārangi ipurangi, heoi ko ngā whārangi o mua ka waiho ki te ipurangi hei tuhinga hukihuki.

source: Blogs can also help improve student writing. While students often begin their blog experience with sloppy grammar and spelling, the presence of an audience generally changes that.
reference: He pai ngā rangitaki ki te whakakoi ake i ngā pukenga tuhi. Ahakoa ngā hapa, ka rerekē haere tēnei āhuatanga i te hunga pānui.

source: Since students are often the most critical audience, the blog writer begins to strive to improve writing to avoid criticism.
reference: Nā te mea he nui te wā ko ngā ākonga te minenga tino whakahē, ka tīmata te kaituhi rangitaki ki te whai kia whakapai ake i te tuhinga hei karo i te whakahēnga.

source: Also blogging "forces students to become more savvy about the world around them." The need to feed the interest of the audience inspires students to be clever and interesting (Toto, 2004).
reference: Mā te rangitaki hoki "e tuku ai ngā ākonga te mōhio ake mō te ao e noho nei rātou." Ka whakahihikotia ngā ākonga kia koi, kia manawa reka, i runga i te hiahia kia whāngai i te aronga mai o te minenga (Toto, 2004).

source: Blogging is a tool that inspires collaboration, and encourages students to extend learning well beyond the traditional school day.
reference: He rauemi te rangitaki e whakaaweawe ai i te mahi tahi, me te akiaki i ngā ākonga kia whāroa ake te ako i tua atu i tētahi rangi noa i te kura.

source: Appropriate use of blogs "can empower students to become more analytical and critical; through actively responding to Internet materials, students can define their positions in the context of others' writings as well as outline their own perspectives on particular issues (Oravec, 2002).
reference: Mā te tika o te mahi rangitaki "e hihiko ai ngā tauira kia mōhio ki te aromātai, ki te arohaehae; ahakoa e aro kē ana ki ngā āhuatanga ipurangi, ka mōhio ngā tauira kei hea ō rātou whakaaro e haere ana mō te āhua ki ngā tuhinga a ētahi atu, me te waihanga hoki i ō rātou ake tirohanga ki ngā take nei" (Oravec, 2002).

source: Ottawa is Canada's charming, bilingual capital and features an array of art galleries and museums that showcase Canada's past and present.
reference: Ko te tāone matua papai rawa e reorua ana, a Ottowa e whakaatua mai ana i ngā whare tapere toi, ngā whare pupuri tāonga me ngā āhutanga o ōnamata tae noa ki ērā o anamata.

source: Farther south is Niagara Falls and the north is home to the untapped natural beauty of the Muskoka and beyond.
reference: Neke atu ki te tonga ko Niagara Falls, ā, ko te raki te kāinga mō te rerehua māori kāore i te tino mōhiotia o te Muskoka, ki tua atu anō hoki.

source: All these things and more highlight Ontario as what is considered quintessentially Canadian by outsiders.
reference: Hei tā te hunga o waho, katoa ēnei mea e tohu ana ko Ontario he rohe ātaahua rawa atu ki Kānata.

source: Large areas further north are quite sparsely populated and some is nearly uninhabited wilderness.
reference: Ko ngā wāhi nui kei te raki e kōpā ana te noho a te tangata, engari anō ngā wāhi rō ngahere, kāore nei e tino pērā rawa.

source: For a comparison of population that surprises many: There are more African Americans living in the US than there are Canadian citizens.
reference: Mō tētahi whakatauritenga o te taupori ka whakaohorere i te tokomaha: He nui ake ngā African Americans e noho ana ki Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika i ngā kirirarau nō Kānata.

source: The East African Islands are in the Indian Ocean off the eastern coast of Africa.
reference: Kei te Indian Ocean ngā motu o Aherika ki te Tonga, kei tua mai o te takutai ki te rāwhiti o Awherika.

source: Madagascar is by far the biggest, and a continent on its own when it comes to wildlife.
reference: Ko Marakāhia te mea nui rawa, ā, he paparahi ki tōna kotahi e hāngai ana ki ngā kararehe mohoao.

source: Most of the smaller islands are independent nations, or associated with France, and known as luxury beach resorts.
reference: He whenua motuhake te nuinga o ngā motu iti iho, he hononga ki Wīwī rānei, ā, kei te mōhiotia hei wāhi whakatā taha moana hāneanea rawa.

source: The Arabs also brought Islam to the lands, and it took in a big way in the Comoros and Mayotte.
reference: Nā ngā Arapi ngā Ihirama i heri mai ki ēnei whenua, ā, he nui te āhua i ahu atu ki ngā Comoro me ngā Mayotte.

source: European influence and colonialism began in the 15th century, as Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama found the Cape Route from Europe to India.
reference: I tīmata te pānga me te mahi koroniara Ūropi i te rautau 15, i te rapunga o te kaihōpara nō Potukara a Vasco da Gama i te Cape Route mai i Ūropi ki Īnia.

source: In the north the region is bounded by the Sahel, and in the south and west by the Atlantic Ocean.
reference: Kei te raki ka noho te Sahel ki te taha o te takiwā, ā, ki te tonga, ki te hauāuru hoki te Atlantic Ocean.

source: Women: It is recommended that any women travellers say that they are married, regardless of actual marital status.
reference: Wāhine: E tautuhia ana kia kīia e te wāhine hāereere kua mārena rātou, ahakoa kua mārena, kāore rānei.

source: It is helpful to also wear a ring (just not one that looks too expensive.
reference: He mea tautoko te mau hoki i tētahi ringi (engari kia kaua tētahi he nui te utu o tōna āhua.

source: Women should realize that cultural differences may result in what they would consider harassment and it is not uncommon to be followed, grabbed by the arm, etc.
reference: Me mōhio te wahine he rerekētanga ahurea ka ahu atu pea ki ngā mahi e whai whakaarotia ana e rātou he whakatīwheta, ā, ka whāia, ka mauria pea te ringa, aha atu.

source: Be firm in turning down men, and don't be afraid to stand your ground (cultural differences or not, it doesn't make it ok!).
reference: Kia kaha ki te whakakahore i te tāne, ā, kaua e mataku ki te tū pakari ai (ahakoa ngā rerekētanga ahurea, kāore tonu i te pai!).

source: The modern city of Casablanca was founded by Berber fishermen in the 10th century BCE, and was used by the Phoenicians, Romans, and the Merenids as a strategic port called Anfa.
reference: I whakatūria te tāone nui hou o Casablanca e ngā kaihī ika Berber i te rau tau tuatekau BCE, ā, i whakamahia e te hunga Phoenician, Rōmana, Merenid anō hoki hei tētahi wāpu rautaki ko Anfa te ingoa.

source: The Portuguese destroyed it and rebuilt it under the name Casa Branca, only to abandon it after an earthquake in 1755.
reference: Nā te iwi Potugese i turaki, ka hanga mai anō me te ingoa o Casa Branca, ka whakarerea hoki whai muri mai i te rū whenua i te tau 1755.

source: The Moroccan sultan rebuilt the city as Daru l-Badya and it was given the name Casablanca by Spanish traders who established trading bases there.
reference: I hangaia anō e te sultan o Marako te tāone nui hei Daru I-Badya, ā, i tapaina ki te ingoa o Casablanca e ngā kaihokohoko Pāniora nā rātou he wāhi hokohoko i whakatū ki reira.

source: Casablanca is one of the least interesting places to shop in all of Morocco.
reference: Ko Casablanca tētahi o ngā wāhi iti rawa te ngahau hei hokohoko ki te katoa o Marako.

source: Around the old Medina it's easy to find places selling traditional Moroccan goods, such as tagines, pottery, leather goods, hookahs, and a whole spectrum of geegaws, but it's all for the tourists.
reference: Huri noa i old Medina he māmā te rapu wāhi e hoko ana i ngā rawa tuku iho Māroko, pērā i te tagine, i te uku, i ngā rawa kirikau, i ngā hookah, i te whānuitanga o ngā geegaws, engari mā te tūruhi te katoa.

source: Goma is a tourist city of the Democratic Republic of Congo in the extreme east near Rwanda.
reference: He tāone tūruhi a Goma o te Democratic Republic of Congo i te rāwhiti rā anō tata ki Rwanda.

source: In 2002 Goma was destroyed by lava from the Nyiragongo volcano which buried most of the town’s streets, particularly the town centre.
reference: I te tau 2002 i horo a Goma i te tahepuia mai i te maunga puia o Nyiragongo, ā, nā tērā i tanu te nuinga o ngā tiriti o te tāone, inarā te pokapū o te tāone.

source: While Goma is reasonably safe, any visits outside of Goma should be researched to understand the state of the fighting that persists in the North Kivu province.
reference: Ahakoa he āhua haumaru a Goma, me rangahau ngā toronga kei waho i Goma hei mārama ki te āhua o te whawhai e haere tonu ana ki te takiwā North Kivu.

source: The city is also the base to climb the Nyiragongo volcano along with some of the cheapest Mountain Gorilla tracking in Africa.
reference: Ko te tāone te pūtake hoki hei piki i te rangitoto, i a Nyiragongo, kei reira hoki ētahi o ngā whaiwhai maunga korira iti te utu o Āwherika.

source: You can use boda-boda (motorcycle taxi) to get around Goma. The normal (local) price is ~500 Congolese Francs for the short ride.
reference: Ka taea e koe te eke i ngā boda-boda (tākihi motopāika) hei huri rāuna i a Goma. Ko te utu noa (ki reira) he ~500 Congolese Francs mō te haerenga iti.

source: Combined with its relative inaccessibility, "Timbuktu" has come to be used as a metaphor for exotic, distant lands.
reference: Hui tahi ki tōna āhua kore āhei atu, kua whakamahia a "Timbuktu" hei kupu whakarite mō te whenua tauhou, tawhiti hoki.

source: Today, Timbuktu is an impoverished town, although its reputation makes it a tourist attraction, and it has an airport.
reference: I tēnei wā, he tāone tino pōhara a Timbuktu, ahakoa he whakamaneatanga tūruhi i runga i tōna rongo, ā, he tauranga waka rererangi ki reira.

source: In 1990, it was added to the list of world heritage sites in danger, due to the threat of desert sands.
reference: I te tau 1990, i tāpiritia ia ki te rārangi o ngā wāhi world heritage e mōrearea ana, i runga i te whakatuma o ngā oneone koraha.

source: It was one of the major stops during Henry Louis Gates' PBS special Wonders of the African World.
reference: Koirā tētahi o ngā tūnga matua i te wā o te PBS special Wonders of the African World o Henry Louis Gates.

source: The city is in stark contrast to the rest of the country's cities, because it has more of an Arabic flair than of an African.
reference: He tīno rerekē te tāone i te toinga atu o ngā tāone o te whenua, i te mea he tāera Arabi tēnā i te Awherika.

source: The Kruger National Park (KNP) lies in the north-east of South Africa and runs along the border of Mozambique in the east, Zimbabwe in the north, and the southern border is the Crocodile River.
reference: Kei te raki mā rāwhiti o Āwherika ki te Tonga te Kruger National Park (KNP), ā, kei te pae o Mozambique ki te rāwhiti, ki Zimbabwe ki te raki, ā, ko te pae tonga ko te Crocodile River.

source: The park covers 19,500 km² and is divided in 14 different ecozones, each supporting different wildlife.
reference: Kei te hōkai 19,500 km₂ te rahi o te pāka, ā, kei te whakawehea ki ngā wāhi rauropi 14 rerekē, kei te tautīnei ia wāhi i ngā karerehe rerekē.

source: It is one of the main attractions of South Africa and it is considered the flagship of South African National Parks (SANParks).
reference: Koirā tētahi o ngā mea whakamanea matua o Awherika ki te Tonga, ā, e whai whakaarohia ana ia hei tohu matua o ngā South African National Parks (SANParks).

source: As with all South African National Parks, there are daily conservation and entry fees for the park.
reference: Mō ngā Rohenga Wao Nui o Aherika ki te Tonga, he utu e hāngai ana i te urunga ki ngā rohenga.

source: It may also be beneficial for one to buy a Wild Card, which provides entry to either selections of parks in South Africa or all of the South African National Parks.
reference: He whaihua pea kia hokona tētahi Wild Card, māna e tuku te kuhunga atu ki ētahi kōwhiringa o ngā pāka ki Āwherika ki te Tonga, ki te katoa rānei o ngā South African National Parks.

source: Hong Kong Island gives the territory of Hong Kong its name and is the place that many tourists regard as the main focus.
reference: Nā Hong Kong Island te ingoa o te rohe o Hong Kong, ā, koirā te wāhi ka whakaarohia e ngā tini tūruhi hei aronga matua.

source: The parade of buildings that make the Hong Kong skyline has been likened to a glittering bar chart that is made apparent by the presence of the waters of Victoria Harbour.
reference: Kua kōrerotia te tini o ngā whare nunui e hanga ai i te huapae o Hong Kong anō nei ko te pae mahere pīataata e mōhio whānuitia ana e ngā wai o Victoria Harbour.

source: To get the best views of Hong Kong, leave the island and head for the Kowloon waterfront opposite.
reference: Ki te kite i ngā tirohanga rawe rawa atu o Hong Kong, me wehe i te motu ka haere ai ki te tāhuna Kowloon kei tērā atu taha.

source: The great majority of Hong Kong Island's urban development is densely packed on reclaimed land along the northern shore.
reference: Kei te kiato te nuinga o te whanaketanga ā-tāone o Hong Kong Island ki te whenua taumanu ki te taha o te tai raki.

source: This is the place the British colonisers took as their own and so if you are looking for evidence of the territory's colonial past, this is a good place to start.
reference: Koinei te wāhi i tangohia e te hunga koroniara nō Peretānia mō rātou anō, nō reira ki te rapu koe i te taunakitanga o te hītori koroniara o te takiwā, koinei tētahi wāhi pai hei tīmata.

source: The Sundarbans are the largest littoral mangrove belt in the world, stretching 80 km (50 mi) into the Bangladeshi and Indian hinterland from the coast.
reference: Ko ngā Sundarbans ngā tātua ritora mangrove nui rawa i te ao, e toro ana i te 80 km (50 mairo) ki roto ki te Bangladeshi me te Iniana whenuaroto tae atu ki te takutai.

source: The Sundarbans has been declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The part of the forest within Indian territory is called Sundarbans National Park.
reference: I ehakatauhia ko te Sundarbans he UNESCO World Heritage Site. Ko te ingoa o te wāhanga ngahere kei te rohe Inia, ko Sundarbans National Park.

source: The forests aren't just mangrove swamps though — they include some of the last remaining stands of the mighty jungles which once covered the Gangetic plain.
reference: Ehara noa ngā ngahengahe nei i te repo waikure - kei roto ētahi o ngā tūnga whakamutunga o te ngahengahe nunui i kapi ai i te raorao Gangetic.

source: The Sundarbans cover an area of 3,850 km², of which about one-third is covered in water/marsh areas.
reference: Kei te horahanga o te 3,850 km2 ngā Sundarbans, ā, ko te kotahi-hautoru e whakakapia ana ki ngā wāhi wai/kōreporepo.

source: Since 1966 the Sundarbans have been a wildlife sanctuary, and it is estimated that there are now 400 Royal Bengal tigers and about 30,000 spotted deer in the area.
reference: Mai i te 1966 kua noho iho te Sundarbans hei whakamaurutanga mohoao, ā, e ai ki te kōrero he 400 ngā tāika ariki Bengal me ngā tia korotiwhatiwha e 30,000 i roto i te wāhi.

source: Buses depart the inter-district bus station (across the river) throughout the day, though most, especially those heading to the east and Jakar/Bumthang leave between 06:30 and 07:30.
reference: Ka wehe ngā pahi i te teihana kei te pokapū (kei tērā atu taha o te awa) i te wā e awatea ana, ahakoa ko te nuinga, mātua rā, ko ērā e haere whaka te raki ana, tae atu anō hoki ki a Jakar/Bumthang ka wehe mai i te 6.30 ki te 7.30.

source: As the inter-district buses are often full, it is advisable to purchase a ticket a few days in advance.
reference: I runga i te noho kī o ngā pahi waenga-rohe i te nuinga o ngā wā, he rautaki pai te hoko tīkiti ētahi rā i mua.

source: Most districts are served by small Japanese Coaster Buses, which are comfortable and sturdy.
reference: Kei te nuinga o ngā rohe ko ngā Japanese Coaster Buses paku, he āhuru, he pakari anō hoki.

source: Shared taxis are a quick and comfortable means to travel to nearby places, such as Paro (Nu 150) and Punakha (Nu 200).
reference: He tere ake, he hāneanea ake te tuari tēkehi ki te haereere ki ngā wahi e tata ana, pērā ki a Paro (Nu 150) me Punkha (Nu 200).

source: The Oyapock River Bridge is a cable-stayed bridge. It spans the Oyapock River to link the cities of Oiapoque in Brazil and Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock in French Guiana.
reference: He piriti here-taura te Oyapock River Bridge. Ka whakawhiti atu i te Oyapock River hei tūhono i ngā tāone nui o Oiapoque ki Parīhi, me Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock ki French Guiana.

source: The two towers rise to a height of 83 meters, it's 378 meters long and it has two lanes of 3.50 m wide.
reference: Ka piki te teitei o ngā pourewa e rua ki te 83 mita, ā, e 378 mita te roa, e rua ngā ara o te 3.50 m te whānui.

source: The vertical clearance under the bridge is 15 meters. Construction was completed in August 2011, it didn't open to traffic until March 2017.
reference: He 15 mita te poutū māhea o raro iho i te piriti. I oti te hanganga i te Ākuhata 2011, kāore i whakatuwhera mō ngā waka tae rawa ki te marama o Māehe i te tau 2017.

source: The bridge is scheduled to be fully operational in September 2017, when the Brazilian customs checkpoints are expected to be finished.
reference: Kua whakaritea kia pai katoa ngā mahi a te piriti hei te Mahuru o te tau 2017, hei taua wā kua oti pea ngā wāhi tirotiro hei ārai o Parihi.

source: The Guaraní were the most significant indigenous group inhabiting what is now Eastern Paraguay, living as semi-nomadic hunters who also practised subsistence agriculture.
reference: Ko te Guarani te iwi taketake hira rawa e noho ana ki te whenua e kīia ana ko Eastern Paraguay ināianei, i te noho hei kaiaruaru āhua-tipiwhenua, i te mahi hoki i te ahuwhenua mā rātou anō.

source: The Chaco region was home to other groups of indigenous tribes such as the Guaycurú and Payaguá, who survived by hunting, gathering and fishing.
reference: I nōhia ai te rohe Chaco e ngā iwi taketake pērā ki ngā Guaycur'u me ngā Payagu'a, i ora ai rātou i te whai kararehe me te hī ika.

source: In the 16th century Paraguay, formerly called "The Giant Province of the Indies", was born as a result of the encounter of Spanish conquerors with the native indigenous groups.
reference: I te rautau 16, i whānau mai a Paraguay, ko tōna ingoa kārangaranga hoki ko "Te Rohe nui o ngā Indies," i ngā taukumekume i ngā waenganui i ngā kairaupatu o Pāniora me ngā iwi taketake.

source: The Spaniards started the colonization period which lasted for three centuries.
reference: I tīmata te iwi Pāniora ki ngā mahi raupatu, toru rautau te roa.

source: Since the foundation of Asunción in 1537, Paraguay has managed to keep a lot of its indigenous character and identity.
reference: Mai i te orokohanganga mai o Asunciōn i te 1537, kia kaha a Paraguay ki te mau tonu ki te nuinga o ngā tāngata whenua me tōna tuakiri.

source: Argentina is well known for having one of the best polo teams and players in the world.
reference: E mōhio whānuitia nei a Argentina mō tētahi o ngā tīma me ngā kaitākaro papai rawa o te ao mō te polo.

source: The largest tournament of the year takes place in December at the polo fields in Las Cañitas.
reference: Ko te whakataetae nui rawa o te tau ka tū i te marama o Tīhema i ngā papa polo i Las Nanitas.

source: Smaller tournaments and matches can also be seen here at other times of the year.
reference: Ka kitea hoki ngā taiwhanga iti me ngā whakataetae ki konei i ētahi atu wāhanga o te tau.

source: For news on tournaments and where to buy tickets for polo matches, check Asociacion Argentina de Polo.
reference: Mō ngā pūrongo e pā ana ki ngā tauwhāinga me ngā wāhi hoko tikiti mō ngā kēmu pōro, tirohia Asociacion Argentina de Polo.

source: The official Falklands currency is the Falkland pound (FKP) whose value is set equivalent to that of one British pound (GBP).
reference: Ko te pūtea tūturu ki te whēnua Falklands ko pāuna Faulkland (FKP), ko tōna wāriu e taurite ana ki te tāra o Ingarangi (GBP).

source: Money can be exchanged at the only bank in the islands which is located in Stanley across from the FIC West store.
reference: Ka taea te whakawhiti i ngā moni ki ngā moutere, kei Stanley, kei tua mai i te toa FIC West.

source: British pounds will generally be accepted anywhere in the islands and within Stanley credit cards and United States dollars are also often accepted.
reference: Ka whakaaetia whānuitia ngā pauna Peretānia puta noa i ngā motu, ā, ki roto tonu i Stanley ka whakaaetia nuitia ngā kāri nama me ngā tāra o Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika.

source: On the outlying islands credit cards will probably not be accepted, although British and United States currency may be taken; check with the owners in advance to determine what is an acceptable payment method.
reference: Ki ngā motu matara kāore pea ngā kāri nama e whakaaetia, ahakoa ka pai pea ngā moni o Peretānia, o Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika hoki; pātai atu ki ngā kaipupuri i mua atu hei whakatau he aha te tikanga utu e whakaaetia ana.

source: It is nearly impossible to exchange Falklands currency outside of the islands, so exchange money prior to leaving the islands.
reference: Kāore nei tonu e taea te whakawhiti i te pūtea Falklands ki waho atu o te moutere, nā reira me whakawhiti koe i ō moni i mua i tō wehenga i ngā moutere.

source: Since Montevideo is south of the Equator, it is summer there when it's winter in the Northern Hemisphere and vice versa.
reference: I te mea kei te tonga a Montevideo i te weheruatanga o te ao, i ngā wā e raumati ana ki reira, e takurua ana ki te Raki, ā huri kau ake hoki.

source: Montevideo is in the subtropics; in the summer months, temperatures above +30°C are common.
reference: Kei ngā takiwā pārū a Montevideo; i ngā marama o te raumati ka eke noa ake i te +30 tākiri C te pāmahana.

source: The winter can be deceptively chilly: temperatures rarely go below freezing, but the wind and humidity combine to make it feel colder than what the thermometer says.
reference: He māminga pea te makariri o te hōtoke: kāore ngā paemahana i te tino heke iho ki raro i te kore, engari ka pāhekoheko te hau me te mākū kia makariri iho te rongo i tā te ine mahana.

source: There are no particular "rainy" and "dry" seasons: the amount of rain stays roughly the same throughout the year.
reference: Kāore he wā ua, kāore he wā maroke: i te roanga o te tau ka pērā tonu te nui o te ua.

source: Though many of the animals in the park are used to seeing humans, the wildlife is nonetheless wild and should not be fed or disturbed.
reference: He ahakoa e waia ana ngā tini kararehe ki te kite atu i te tangata, he niwhaniwha tonu ngā karerehe , ā, me kauaka e whāngai e whakahōhā rānei.

source: According to park authorities, stay at least 100 yards/meters away from bears and wolves and 25 yards/meters from all other wild animals!
reference: E ai ki ngā mana o te pāka, me noho tonu te 100 iari/mita atu i ngā pea me ngā wuruhi me te 25 iari/mita atu i ērā atu kararehe mohoao katoa!

source: No matter how docile they may look, bison, elk, moose, bears, and nearly all large animals can attack.
reference: he ahakoa pēhea nei te kōtungatunga o te āhua, e āhei ana te pūrununui, te heka, te Tianui, te pea te āki.

source: Each year, dozens of visitors are injured because they didn't keep a proper distance. These animals are large, wild, and potentially dangerous, so give them their space.
reference: tau atu tau mai ka aituangia ngā tūruhi i te mea kāore i tika te tawhititanga. He nunui ēnei kararehe, he pāwhara, he mōrearea hoki pea, nāreira hoatu he maheatanga ki a rātou.

source: In addition, be aware that odors attract bears and other wildlife, so avoid carrying or cooking odorous foods and keep a clean camp.
reference: Hei āpiti, kia kamakama ka hau mai ngā pea me ngā atu kararehe puihi i te mea o ngā kakara, nā me ngana kia kauka e kawe e tunu rānei i ngā kai kakara, ā, me mā hoki te puni.

source: Apia is the capital of Samoa. The town is on the island of Upolu and has a population of just under 40,000.
reference: Ko Apia te tāone matua o Hāmoa. Kei te motu o Upolu te tāone, ā, he rahinga tāngata paku iti iho i te 40,000.

source: Apia was founded in the 1850s and has been the official capital of Samoa since 1959.
reference: I whakatūria a Apia i ngā tau 1850,, ā, koirā te tāone matua ōkawa o Hāmoa mai i te tau 1959.

source: The harbor was the site of an infamous naval standoff in 1889 when seven ships from Germany, the US, and Britain refused to leave the harbor.
reference: He wāhi mōiriiri te tauranga nei o tētahi pakanga tauā moana e mōhiotia ana i te kino i te tau 1889 i te wā i whanakanau ngā kaipuke e whitu mai i Tiamani, i Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika, i Peretānia anō hoki ki te wehe atu i te tauranga.

source: All the ships were sunk, except for one British cruiser. Nearly 200 American and German lives were lost.
reference: I te totohu o ngā kaipuke katoa, atu i te kaipuke hāereere kotahi nō Peretānia. Tata ki te 200 ngā tāngata no Amerika, nō Tiamani hoki i mate.

source: During the struggle for independence organised by the Mau movement, a peaceful gathering in the town resulted in the killing of the paramount chief Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.
reference: I te wā o te uauatanga mō te tīno rangatiratanga te take, ko te rōpū Mau te rōpū whakahaere. I roto i te huinga mō te rongomau ka mate te ariki a Tupua Tamasese Lealofi te tuatoru.

source: There are many beaches, due to Auckland's straddling of two harbours. The most popular ones are in three areas.
reference: He maha ngā tuaone, i runga i te noho waenga a Tāmaki i ngā whanga e rua. Ko ngā mea rorotu kei ngā wāhi e toru.

source: North Shore beaches (in North Harbour district) are on the Pacific Ocean and stretch from Long Bay in the north to Devonport in the south.
reference: Ko ngā takutai ki Te Raki Paewhenua (kei Te Whanganui a Toi) kei Te Moana nui a Kiwa, e toro ana mai i Long Bay ki Devonport.

source: They are almost all sandy beaches with safe swimming, and most have shade provided by pohutukawa trees.
reference: Ko te nuinga o ngā moana haumaru nei, ā, ko te nuinga hoki kua whakamarua e te rākau pōhutukawa.

source: Tamaki Drive beaches are on the Waitemata Harbour, in the upmarket suburbs of Mission Bay and St Heliers in Central Auckland.
reference: Kei te Pūahatanga Waitemata ngā taha moana o Tamaki Drive, ki ngā wāhi tāone whai rawa o Mission Bay me St Heliers ki te pito o Tāmaki Makaurau.

source: These are sometimes-crowded family beaches with a good range of shops lining the shore. Swimming is safe.
reference: I ētahi wā he wāhi i kī ana i ngā whānau tēnei moana me ngā tini toa papai e tata ana ki te tai. He kaukau haumaru.

source: The main local beer is 'Number One', it is not a complex beer, but pleasant and refreshing. The other local beer is called "Manta".
reference: Ko te pia ā-rohe matua ko te "Nama tahi", ehara i te pia uaua, ēngari he māma he whakarekareka. Ko te atu pia ā-rohe ko te "Manta"

source: There are many French wines to be had, but the New Zealand and Australian wines might travel better.
reference: He nui ngā waina Wīwī, engari ka whāroa ake pea te totoro atu o ngā waina a Aotearoa me Ahitereiria.

source: The local tap water is perfectly safe to drink, but bottled water is easy to find if you are fearful.
reference: He pai noa iho te inu wai mai i ngā kōrere ki reira, heoi mēnā e wehi ana, he māmā te kite i ngā wai pātara.

source: For Australians, the idea of 'flat white' coffee is foreign. A short black is 'espresso', cappuccino comes heaped high with cream (not froth), and tea is served without milk.
reference: Mo ngā Ahitereiria, ko te ariā mō te kawhe "papatahi mā' he manene. Ko te short black he "espresso", ko te cappuccino he kirimi kei te tihi (ehara i te whorowho), ā, karekau te tī kua ranu me te miraka.

source: The hot chocolate is up to Belgian standards. Fruit juices are pricey but excellent.
reference: He pēnei i te taumata o Belgian te tiakarete wera nei. He nui te utu mō ngā inu huarākau ēngari he tīno reka.

source: Many trips to the reef are made all year around, and injuries due to any of these causes on the reef are rare.
reference: He maha ngā haerenga ki te ākau puta noa i te tau, ā, kāore e kitea whānuitia ana ngā whara i runga i tētahi o ēnei take ki te ākau.

source: Still, take advice from authorities, obey all signs, and pay close attention to safety warnings.
reference: Engari tonu, whāia ngā tohutohu a ngā rangatita, me aro hoki ki ngā tohu haumaru.

source: Box jellyfish occur near beaches and near river estuaries from October to April north of 1770. They can occasionally be found outside these times.
reference: Ka kitea ngā momo Tīwai nei ki tātahi me te taha o ngā awa mai i te marama o Oketopa me Aperira, ki te raki o 1700. I ētahi ka kitea i waho atu i ēnei wā.

source: Sharks do exist, however they rarely attack humans. Most sharks are scared of humans and would swim away.
reference: He mea tūturu ngā mangō, heoi anō he iti rawa tā rātou whakaeke i te tangata. Kei te mataku te nuinga o ngā mangō i te tangata, ka kaukau noa atu.

source: Saltwater Crocodiles do not actively live in the ocean, their primary habitat is in river estuaries north from Rockhampton.
reference: Kāore ngā waitā korokotaira e tīno noho ki rō moana, ko tō rātou tīno wāhi noho ko ngā awaawa kei te raki o Rockhampton.

source: Booking in advance gives the traveller peace of mind that they will have somewhere to sleep once they arrive at their destination.
reference: Mā te whakarite i mua e tau ai te hinengaro a te tangata hāereere he wāhi māna hei moe ina tae atu ki te wāhi rā.

source: Travel agents often have deals with specific hotels, although you may find it possible to book other forms of accommodation, like camping grounds, through a travel agent.
reference: He nui ngā wā he whakaritenga ā ngā kaiwhakarite haerenga ki ētahi hōtēra, ahakoa ka taea pea e koe te whakarite i ētahi momo noho, pērā i ngā puninga, mā tētahi kaiwhakarite haerenga.

source: Travel agents usually offer packages that include breakfast, transportation arrangements to/from the airport or even combined flight and hotel packages.
reference: He rite tonu te tuku a ngā kaiwhakarite hāereere i ngā kete kei roto rā ko te parakuihi, ngā whakaritenga ikiiki ki/atu i te tauranga wakarererangi, ngā kete paheko i te rerenga me te hōtēra rānei.

source: They can also hold the reservation for you if you need time to think about the offer or procure other documents for your destination (e.g. visa).
reference: Ka taea hoki e rātou te pupuri i te whakaritenga māu ki te hiahia wā koe hei whakaaro ki te tukunga, ki te whiwhi rānei i ētahi atu puka mō te wāhi ka torohia e koe (hei tauira, he pane uruuruwhenua).

source: Any amendments or requests though should be coursed through the travel agent first and not directly with the hotel.
reference: Tukuna ngā whakatikatika me ngā tono ki te kaitiaki haereere i te tuatahi, kaua ko te hōtēra.

source: For some festivals, the vast majority of the attendants to music festivals decide to camp on site, and most attendants consider it a vital part of the experience.
reference: Mō ētahi taiopenga, ko te nuinga o ngā kaitautoko o ngā huihuinga waiata nei ka hiahia ki te whakapuni ki rō papa, ā, ko te nuinga o ngā kaitautoko ka whakatau koinaka tētahi o ngā tīno wāhanga o te wheakoranga.

source: If you want to be close to the action you're going to have to get in early to get a camping site close to the music.
reference: Me kei te hiahia koe kia tata atu ki ngā mahi, me tae wawe atu koe kia whai wāhi atu ki tētahi puni tata ki ngā waiata.

source: Remember that even though music on the main stages may have finished, there may be sections of the festival that will keep playing music until late into the night.
reference: Me maumahara ahakoa kua mutu pea te puoro ki ngā atamira matua, tērā ētahi wāhi o te hui ahurei e whakatangi puoro tai nuaku ana kia waenganui pō.

source: Some festivals have special camping areas for families with young children.
reference: Kei ētahi hui ahurei he wāhi tēneti motuhake mā ngā whānau whai tamariki nohinohi.

source: If crossing the Northern Baltic in winter, check the cabin location, as going through ice causes quite horrible noise for those most affected.
reference: Mēnā e whakawhiti ana koe i te awa ki Northen Baltic, tirohia ngā wāhi hōpuni i te mea ko te haere i roto i te huka he hoihoi mō te hunga ka pāngia e tērā momo mate.

source: Saint Petersburg cruises include time in town. Cruise passengers are exempted from visa requirements (check the terms).
reference: Kei roto i ngā rerenga Saint Petersburg he wā ki rō tāone. Kei te wātea ngā pāhihi rerenga i ngā herenga pane uruwhenua (tirohia ngā herenga).

source: Casinos typically make many efforts to maximize time and money spent by guests. Windows and clocks are usually absent, and exits can be hard to find.
reference: I ētahi wā ka whakapau kaha ngā whare petipeti kia nui ake ai te wā me te moni e whakapaungia ana e ngā manuhiri. I te nuinga o te wā kāore i te kitea ngā wini, ngā karaka rānei, ā, he uaua te kitea o ngā putanga.

source: They usually have special food, drink and entertainment offers, to keep guests in a good mood, and keep them at the premise.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā he ratonga kai, inu, whakangahau motuhake, kia noho hari tonu ngā manuhiri, kia noho tonu rātou ki te whare.

source: Some venues offer alcoholic beverages on the house. However, drunkenness impairs judgement, and all good gamblers know the importance of staying sober.
reference: Tērā ētahi hingonga ka koha atu i te inu waipiro. Heoi, ka riro te tangata i roto i tana haurangi, kei te mōhio hoki ngā kaipetipeti toa ki te hirahiratanga o te noho haurangi kore.

source: Anyone who's going to drive at high latitudes or over mountain passes should consider the possibility of snow, ice, or freezing temperatures.
reference: Me whai whakaaro te tangata ka taraiwa ake ki ngā ahopae teitei, ki ngā tarahanga maunga rānei kei pā pea te hukarere, te tio, ngā paemahana tino makariri rānei.

source: On icy and snowy roadways, friction is low and you cannot drive as if you were on bare asphalt.
