reference: Ki ngā huarahi tio, hukapapa hoki, he iti te pane uruwhenua, ā, e kore e taea e koe te taraiwa anō nei kei te raima kau koe.

source: During blizzards, enough snow to get you stuck can fall in very little time.
reference: I ngā huka kairākau, ka tau pea te nui o ngā huka kia mau koe i te wā poto rawa.

source: Visibility may also be restricted by falling or blowing snow or by condensation or ice on vehicle windows.
reference: Ka whakawhāititia hoki pea te ari e te huka rere, te huka pupuhi rānei, e te tōtā, te tio rānei ki ngā matapihi o te waka.

source: On the other hand, icy and snowy conditions are normal in many countries, and traffic goes on mostly uninterrupted all year round.
reference: Ki tērā atu taha, he noa ngā āhuatanga tio, hukapapa hoki ki ngā tini whenua, ā, ka haere tonu te ikiiki kāore he tino raru puta noa i te tau.

source: Safaris are perhaps the greatest tourism draw in Africa and the highlight for many visitors.
reference: Ākuanei pea ko ngā Safari ngā tino mahinga tūruhi ki Awherika me te tīno mahinga mō te tini o ngā tūruhi.

source: The term safari in popular use refers to overland travel to view the stunning African wildlife, particularly on savanna.
reference: Ko te kupu safari e whakamahi whanuingia ana hei āhuatanga haerere ki uta ki tai ki te titiro ki ngā kararehe mohoao o Awherika, inā koā ki te wāhanga Savanna.

source: Some animals, such as elephants and giraffes, tend to approach closely to cars and standard equipment will allow good viewing.
reference: Ētahi kararehe pēnei i ngā arewhana me ngā Hirāwhe ka whakatata atu i ētahi wā ki ngā motokā, ā, mā te taputapu ngāwari noa e whai wāhi atu ai ki te tirohanga papai.

source: Lions, cheetahs and leopards are sometimes shy and you will see them better with binoculars.
reference: Ka whakamā i ētahi wā ngā raiona, ngā tīta, ngā rēpara hoki, ā, ka pai ake tō kite i a rātou ki ngā karu whakatata.

source: A walking safari (also called a "bush walk", "hiking safari", or going "footing") consists of hiking, either for a few hours or several days.
reference: Ko tētahi safari hīkoi (e kīia ana hoki tētahi "hīkoi ngahere", "safari takahi whenua", te haere "mā raro" rānei) ko te hīkoi, mō ētahi hāora, ētahi rangi rānei.

source: The Paralympics will take place from 24 August to 5 September 2021. Some events will be held in other locations throughout Japan.
reference: Ka tū Ngā Taumāhekeheke o te Ao mō te Hunga Pararūtiki atu i te 24 o Ākuhata ki te 5 o Hepetema hei te tau 2021. Ka tū ētahi o ngā mahi ki wāhi kē atu puta noa i Hapani.

source: Tokyo will be the only Asian city to have hosted two summer Olympics, having hosted the games in 1964.
reference: Ka tū a Tokyo hei tāone nui kotahi nō Āhia kia manaaki i ngā Taumāhekeheke o te Ao ā-raumati e rua, i runga i tana manaaki i ngā taumāhekeheke i te tau 1964.

source: If you booked your flights and accommodation for 2020 before the postponement was announced, you may have a tricky situation.
reference: Mēnā i hoko koe i ō rerenga, i tō whare noho mō te tau 2020 i mua i te whakapuakitanga o te takaroatanga, ka raru pea.

source: Cancellation policies vary, but as of late March most coronavirus-based cancellation policies don't extend to July 2020, when the Olympics had been scheduled.
reference: Ka rerekē ngā kaupapa here whakakore, ēngari i te mutunga o te Māihe ko te nuinga o ngā kaupapa here whakakorenga huaketo kāore i toro ki te Hurae 2020, i te wā i whakaritea mai ai ngā kēmu taumāhekeheke o te ao.

source: It's expected that most event tickets will cost between ¥2,500 and ¥130,000, with typical tickets costing around ¥7,000.
reference: Ka whakaarotia ko te utu o te nuinga o ngā tīkiti whakaaturanga kei waenganui i te 2,500 yen me te 130,000 yen, ā, ko ngā tīkiti whānui he tata ki te 7,000 yen.

source: Ironing damp clothes can help them dry. Many hotels have an iron and ironing board available for loan, even if one is not present in the room.
reference: Mā te haeana kākahu mākū e āwhina pea te whakamaroke. He maha ngā hōtēra whai haeana me te papa haeana e wātea ana ki te whakamahi, ahakoa kāore tētahi ki te ruma.

source: If an iron isn't available, or if you don't fancy wearing ironed socks, then you can try using a hairdryer, if available.
reference: Ki te kore he aiana, ki te kore rānei koe e pai ki te mau i ngā tōkena iana, tēnā, e taea nei te whakamahi i tētahi mihini whakamaroke makawe mēnā kei reira tētahi.

source: Be careful not to allow fabric to become too hot (which can cause shrinkage, or in extreme cases, scorch).
reference: Kia tūpato kia kaua e tuku i te kākahu kia wera rawa (mā korā pea e pīngongo, i ētahi wā kino ka hunuhunutia rānei).

source: There are different ways of purifying water, some more effective against specific threats.
reference: He ara rerekē hei whakamā i te wai, ka pai ake ētahi ki ētahi whakatuma ake.

source: In some areas boiling water for a minute is enough, in others several minutes are needed.
reference: I ētahi wāhi he nawhe te koropupū i te wai mō te kotahi miniti, i ētahi atu wāhi me nui atu ngā miniti.

source: Filters vary in effectiveness, and should you have a concern, then you should consider buying your water in a sealed bottle from a reputable company.
reference: Ka rerekē te painga o ngā tātari, ā, mehemea he āwangawanga tāu, me whai whakaaro koe ki te hoko i tō wai ki tētahi pātara kua kōpanitia mai i tētahi kamupene e mōhiotia ana.

source: Travellers may encounter animal pests that they are not familiar with in their home regions.
reference: Ka kite pea te hunga hāereere i ngā riha kararehe kāore i te waia ki ēnei ki ō rātou wā kāinga.

source: Pests can spoil food, cause irritation, or in a worse case cause allergic reactions, spread venom, or transmit infections.
reference: Ka taea e ngā riha te whakapirau kai, te whakaputa hīrawerawe, ā, ko te mea kino rawa ka whakaputa mate tāwera, te whakawhānui paitini, te tuku poke rānei.

source: Infectious diseases themselves, or dangerous animals that can injure or kill people by force, do not usually qualify as pests.
reference: Ko ngā mate hōrapa ake, ko ngā kararehe mōrearea rānei ka taea te whara, te whakamate rānei i te tangata mā te tōpana, i te nuinga o te wā kāore i te kīia he kīrearea.

source: Duty free shopping is the opportunity to buy goods exempted from taxes and excises at certain locations.
reference: Ko te hokohoko tāke kore he ara hei hoko i ngā rawa karekau he here ki te tāka e mahia ana ki ētahi wāhi motuhake.

source: Travellers bound for countries with heavy taxation can sometimes save a considerable amount of money, especially on products such as alcoholic beverages and tobacco.
reference: I ētahi wā e āhei ai te hunga hāereere ki ngā motu he nui te tāke kia whakaemi i ētahi moni nui, ā, e pēnei ana hoki mō ngā taputapu pēnei i ngā inu waipiro me ngā tupeka anō hoki.

source: The stretch between Point Marion and Fairmont presents the most challenging driving conditions on the Buffalo-Pittsburgh Highway, passing frequently through isolated backwoods terrain.
reference: Ko te wāhanga i waenganui i Point Marion me Fairmont kei te whakatū i ngā āhuatanga taraiwa uaua rawa ki te Buffalo-Pittsburgh Highway, he rite tonu te haere mā ngā whenua wao mohoao.

source: If you're not used to driving on country roads, keep your wits about you: steep grades, narrow lanes, and sharp curves predominate.
reference: Ki te kore koe e waia ana ki te taraiwa ki ngā huarahi taiwhenua, kia mataara tonu koe: he nui ngā paripari, ngā huarahi whāiti, me ngā kokonga koki tāhapa.

source: Posted speed limits are noticeably lower than in previous and subsequent sections — commonly 35-40 mph (56-64 km/h) — and strict obedience to them is even more important than otherwise.
reference: He iti iho ngā here tere e tohua ana i ērā ki ngā wāhanga o mua, o muri anō hoki - he nui te wā ko te 35-40 m/h (56-64 km/h), - ā, he mea hira rawa te tino whai i ēnei i ērā atu.

source: Curiously, though, mobile phone service is much stronger here than along many other stretches of the route, e.g. the Pennsylvania Wilds.
reference: He mea māhirahira, he kaha ake te ratonga waea pūkoro ki konei i ērā atu roanga o te huarahi, hei tauira ngā Pennsylvania Wilds.

source: German pastries are quite good, and in Bavaria, are quite rich and varied, similar to those of their southern neighbor, Austria.
reference: He āhua reka ngā parāoa Tiamani, ā, ki Bavaria, he āhua mōmona, he nui ngā momo, pērā i ērā o tā rātou whenua tata ki te tonga, o Ateria.

source: Fruit pastries are common, with apples cooked into pastries year round, and cherries and plums making their appearances during the summer.
reference: E kitea noatia ana ngā tāte huarākau, arā ka tunua ngā āporo ki ngā tāte puta noa i te tau, ā, ka kitea ngā tiere, ngā paramu i te wā o te raumati.

source: Many German baked goods also feature almonds, hazelnuts, and other tree nuts. Popular cakes often pair particularly well with a cup of strong coffee.
reference: Kei ngā tini tunumanga parāoa Tiamani he aramona, he hānati, he nati rākau atu anō hoki. He tino pai te kai tahi i ngā keke rorotu ki tētahi kapu kawhe hūnene.

source: If you want some small though rich pastries, try what depending on region are called Berliner, Pfannkuchen or Krapfen.
reference: Me kei te hiahia koe i ētahi paoa rekareka iti nei, taraingia te aha e ai ki te rohe kua ingoa Berliner, Pfannkuchen, Krapfen rānei.

source: A curry is a dish based on herbs and spices, together with either meat or vegetables.
reference: Ko te kai kari tētahi kai kua whakaritea ki ngā otaota, ki ngā rau kikini, tahi ki te mīti, te huawhenua rānei.

source: A curry can be either "dry" or "wet" depending on the amount of liquid.
reference: He "maroke", he "mākū" rānei tētahi kari i runga i te nui o te wē.

source: In inland regions of Northern India and Pakistan, yogurt is commonly used in curries; in Southern India and some other coastal regions of the subcontinent, coconut milk is commonly used.
reference: Ki ngā rohe tuawhenua o Īnia ki te Raki, o Pakitāne, kei te whakamahia whānuitia te miraka tepe ki ngā kari; ki Īnia ki te Tonga, ki ētahi atu takiwā ki tai o te paparahi āpiti, kei te whakamahia whānuitia te miraka kokonati.

source: With 17,000 islands to choose from, Indonesian food is an umbrella term covering a vast variety of regional cuisines found across the nation.
reference: I roto i te 17,000 ngā motu hei kōwhiringa, ko te kai Indonesia hei āhuatanga hamarara e whakakapi nei i te tini i ngā kai taketake ā-rohe e horapa ana i te whenua.

source: But, if used without further qualifiers, the term tends to mean the food originally from the central and eastern parts of the main island Java.
reference: Heoi anō, mehemea ka whakamahia karekau ngā kupu anō, ko te tino tikanga o te kupu ko te kai i ahu mai i ngā wāhi waenga, tawhiti hoki o te motu matua o Java.

source: Now widely available throughout the archipelago, Javanese cuisine features an array of simply seasoned dishes, the predominant flavorings the Javanese favor being peanuts, chillies, sugar (especially Javanese coconut sugar) and various aromatic spices.
reference: Ināianei kei te wātea mai ngā momo kai Javanese puta noa i te huinga motu, ā, kei a ia te whānuitanga o ngā kai he māmā noa te hā, ā, ko ngā hā matua he pai ki te hunga Javanese ko te pīnati, te hiri, te huka (inarā ko te huka kokonati Javanese), me ngā tini rau kikini whakakakara.

source: Stirrups are supports for the rider's feet that hang down on either side of the saddle.
reference: He mea tautoko ngā tarapu mō ngā waewae o te kaieke ka tau ki raro ki ngā taha e rua o te tera.

source: They provide greater stability for the rider but can have safety concerns due to the potential for a rider's feet to get stuck in them.
reference: Ka hoatu rātou he tiakitanga pai ake mō te kaieke ēngari he āwangawangatanga tonu mō te haumarutanga i te mea o ngā waewae o te kaieke kei mau.

source: If a rider is thrown from a horse but has a foot caught in the stirrup, they could be dragged if the horse runs away. To minimize this risk, a number of safety precautions can be taken.
reference: Ki te whiua tētahi kaieke i tētahi hōiho engari kua mau te waewae ki te tarapu, ka tōngia pea mēnā ka oma ake te hōiho. Hei whakaiti rawa i tēnei mōrearea, ka taea ētahi tūpatotanga haumaru.

source: First, most riders wear riding boots with a heel and a smooth, quite narrow, sole.
reference: Mātua, ko te nuinga o ngā kaieke ka mau pūtu tika pōtae tika me ngā mau rekereke tika, me te kuiti māeneene whāiti nei.

source: Next, some saddles, particularly English saddles, have safety bars that allow a stirrup leather to fall off the saddle if pulled backwards by a falling rider.
reference: E whai ake nei ko ētahi terapēke, inarā ko ngā terapēke Ingarihi, he pā marutau ōna e āhei ai te tarapu kirikau te taka i te terapēke mēnā kua tōia whakamuritia e tētahi kai eke e taka ana.

source: Cochamó Valley - Chile's premier climbing destination, known as the Yosemite of South America, with a variety of granite big walls and crags.
reference: Te Taiororua o Cochamō - Te wāhi whakaeke kounga rawa o Hiri, e mōhiotia ana ko te Yosemite o Amerika ki te Tonga, me ana momo pakitara me ngā kōhatu tokapata nunui.

source: Summits include breath-taking views from peaks. Climbers from all parts of the world are continually establishing new routes amongst its endless potential of walls.
reference: Kei roto i te piki ki ngā tihi ko ngā tirohanga mīharo i ngā tihi. Kei te whakatūtū tonu ngā kaipiki mai i ngā tōpito katoa o te ao i ngā ara hou ki ōna pakitara mutunga kore.

source: Downhill snowsports, which include skiing and snowboarding, are popular sports involving sliding down snow-covered terrain with skis or a snowboard attached to your feet.
reference: Ko ngā hākinakina heke haere, kei roto rā ko te retireti me te eke papa hukapapa, he hākinakina makau ko te mahi ko te rere iho i te whenua e kapia ana ki te hukapapa ki ngā retireti, ki tētahi papa hukapapa rānei e tūhonotia ana ki ō waeawae.

source: Skiing is a major travelling activity with many enthusiasts, occasionally known as "ski bums," planning entire vacations around skiing at a particular location.
reference: He hanga haerere te retireti me ngā tini tāngata hikaka, e mōhiotia whānuitia ki te "reti nono", te whakarite i ngā hararei reti i tētahi wāhi motuhake.

source: The idea of skiing is very old — cave paintings depicting skiers date back as far as 5000 BC!
reference: He tawhito rawa te ariā o te eke rēti - kei te kitea he toi ana e whakaatu ana i ngā kaieke rēti mai rā anō i te 5000 BC!

source: Downhill skiing as a sport goes back to at least the 17th century, and in 1861 the first recreational ski club was opened by Norwegians in Australia.
reference: Mai i te rautau tua 17, neke atu rānei, te whakaretireti whakaheke hei hākinakina, ā, i te tau 1861 i whakatuwheratia te karapu retireti tuatahi e ētahi tāngata nō Nōwei ki Ahitereiria.

source: Backpacking by ski: This activity is also called backcountry ski, ski touring or ski hiking.
reference: Paketeke mā te retihuka: ko tēnei mahinga e kī ana hoki ko te reti reti i ngā whenua o muri, ko te reti tūruhi, ko te reti hīkoinga rānei.

source: It is related to but usually not involving alpine style ski touring or mountaineering, the latter ones done in steep terrain and requiring much stiffer skis and boots.
reference: E hāngai ana ki te haere retireti āhua maunga, te piki maunga rānei, engari i te nuinga o te wā kāore i te whai wāhi ēnei, ko ēnei e mahia ana ki ngā wāhi paripari, ā, ka hiahiatia ngā reti me ngā pūtu māro ake.

source: Think of the skiing route as of a similar hiking route.
reference: Whakaarohia te ara retireti hei ara hīkoi ōrite.

source: In good conditions you will be able to cover somewhat greater distances than walking – but only very seldom you will get the speeds of cross country skiing without a heavy backpack in groomed tracks.
reference: I roto i ngā āhuatanga papai ka roa ake ngā roanga ka taea e koe i tā te hīkoi - heoi i ētahi wā iti nei ka tae rawa koe ki te terenga o te retireti hīkoi hukapapa mēnā horekau tētahi pīkau taumaha ki ngā ara kua whakaritea.

source: Europe is a continent that is relatively small but with many independent countries. Under normal circumstances, travelling through multiple countries would mean having to go through visa applications and passport control multiple times.
reference: He iti noa nei te whenua whānui a Ūropi engari he nui ngā iwi motuhake kei reira. Ko tōna āhua, ko te karore i ngā whenua huhua he nui ngā wā me whakamātauria ngā pane uruwhenua e uru ai ki aua whenua rā.

source: The Schengen zone, however, works somewhat like one country in this respect.
reference: Heoi anō, he āhua pērā i tētahi whenua te Schengen zone, mō tēnei āhuatanga.

source: As long as you stay in this zone, you can generally cross borders without going through passport control checkpoints again.
reference: I a koe ka noho ki tēnei rohe, ka taea e koe te whakawhiti i ngā rohe, ā, ehara i te mea me haere anō mā ngā tirotiro kaitiaki uruwhenua.

source: Similarly, by having a Schengen visa, you do not need to apply for visas to each of the Schengen member countries separately, hence saving time, money and paperwork.
reference: Me te mea hoki, mā te mau i tētahi visa Schengen me tono motuhake koe mō ngā visas kei ia o ngā whenua mema Schengen, ā, koira e maha ake te wā, te moni me ngā pepamahi.

source: There is no universal definition for which manufactured items are antiques. Some tax agencies define goods older than 100 years as antiques.
reference: Kāore he mōhiotanga whānui hei tautohu i ngā taputapu hei taputapu nō tua whakarere. Ētahi kaiwhakahaere tāki ka tautohu i ngā taputapu nō tua whakarere mēnā e nui atu i te 100 tau te tawhito.

source: The definition has geographic variations, where the age limit might be shorter in places such as North America than in Europe.
reference: He rerekē ā-matawhenua nei ngā whakamāramatanga, ka rerekē pea te tau ngā tau kei ngā whenua, pērā ki Te Raki o Amerika me Ūropi.

source: Handicraft products might be defined as antiques, though they are younger than similar mass-produced goods.
reference: Ko ngā rawa mahi-a-ringa ka kīa he tāonga nō uki, he ahakoa i hangaia i muri i ētahi tāonga kua whakaputaina nuitia.

source: Reindeer husbandry is an important livelihood among the Sámi and the culture surrounding the trade is important also for many with other professions.
reference: He hirahira te pāmu renetia mō te oranga o te Sāmi, ā, ko te ahurea e hāngai ana ki te hohoko he hirahira ki ngā umanga maha.

source: Even traditionally, though, not all Sámi have been involved in big scale reindeer husbandry, but lived from fishing, hunting and similar, having reindeer mostly as draft animals.
reference: Ahakoa i ngā wā o mua, kāore ngā Sāmi katoa i whai wāhi ki te mahi pāmu nui i ngā renetia, ēngari i ora nā te hī ika, nā te aru, nā ngā mahi pēnei, ko te renetia i whakamahia hei kararehe mahi i te nuinga o te wā.

source: Today many Sámi work in modern trades. Tourism is an important income in Sápmi, the Sámi area.
reference: I ēnei rā nei he nui ngā Sāmi e mahi ana i ngā mahi o ēnei wā nei. Ka nui te moni e ahu mai ana i ngā mahi tūruhi i Sāpmi, te wāhi o Sāmi.

source: Though it is widely used, especially among non-Romani, the word "Gypsy" is often considered offensive because of its associations with negative stereotypes and inaccurate perceptions of Romani people.
reference: He ahakoa e horapa ana te whakamahinga, inarā i waenganui i te hunga ehara i te Romani, e whai whakaarotia te kupu "Gypsy" hei kupu kino i te nuinga o te wā nā tana hononga ki te whakaaro horapa kino me ngā whakaaro hē mō ngā tāngata Romani.

source: If the country you will be visiting becomes subject to a travel advisory, your travel health insurance or your trip cancellation insurance may be affected.
reference: Mehemea ka pā tētahi tohu hāereere ki te whenua e torohia ana e koe, ka pāngia pea tō inihua hauora hāereere, tō inihua whakakore haerenga rānei.

source: You may also wish to consult the advice of governments other than your own, but their advice is designed for their citizens.
reference: Ka hiahia hoki pea koe ki te aro atu ki ngā tohutohu o ngā kāwanatanga atu i tō ake, engari kei te hoahoatia ō rātou tohutohu mō te hunga nō tō rātou whenua ake.

source: As one example, American citizens in the Middle East might face different situations from Europeans or Arabs.
reference: Hei tētahi tauira, ka aro atu pea ngā kainoho nō Amerika ki te Rāwhiti Waenga i ngā āhuatanga rerekē i te hunga nō Ūropi, i ngā Arapa rānei.

source: Advisories are merely a brief summary of the political situation in one country.
reference: Ko ngā kaitohutohu he kōrero whakarāpopoto noa mō ngā āhuatanga tōrangapū o te whenua kotahi.

source: The views presented are often cursory, general and oversimplified compared to the more detailed information available elsewhere.
reference: Ko ngā whakaaro he wairua whakahāwea, he whakaaro whānui rawa, kāore i te hōhonu, kāore i te whāiti te titiro.

source: Severe weather is the generic term for any dangerous weather phenomenon with the potential to cause damage, serious social disruption, or loss of human life.
reference: Ko te huarere kino ko te rerenga noa mō tētahi āhuatanga huarere mōrearea ka whakaritea pea e ia te kino, te whakararu papori nui, te matenga rānei o te tangata.

source: Severe weather can occur anywhere in the world, and there are different types of it, which can depend on geography, topography, and atmospheric conditions.
reference: Ka taea te huarere kino te puta mai ki ngā wāhi katoa o te ao, ā, he maha ngā momo rerekē, ka hāngai pea ki te takotoranga papa, te takoto whenua, me ngā āhuatanga kōhauhau.

source: High winds, hail, excessive precipitation, and wildfires are forms and effects of severe weather, as are thunderstorms, tornadoes, waterspouts, and cyclones.
reference: Ko ngā hau kaha, ko te hukawaitara, ko te ua nui rawa, ko ngā ahi tarakaka ētahi āhua, ētahi pānga o te huarere kino, pērā anō hoki i ngā rautupu, i ngā āwhiowhio, i ngā puahiritanga, i ngā haumātakataka anō hoki.

source: Regional and seasonal severe weather phenomena include blizzards, snowstorms, ice storms, and dust storms.
reference: Ko ngā āhuatanga huarere kino ā-rohe, ā-kaupeka hoki ko ngā huka kairākau, ko ngā ua puahiri, ko ngā paroro tio me ngā paroro kōnehu.

source: Travellers are strongly advised to be aware of any risk of severe weather affecting their area as they may affect any travel plans.
reference: E tīno akiaki ana i ngā tūruhi kia matāra ai rātou i ngā tūraru marangai kei whakararu i tō rātou wāhi i roto anō hoki i te hanga nei ki te whakararu i te haerenga ahakoa te aha.

source: Anyone planning a visit to a country that could be considered a war zone should get professional training.
reference: Me whai whakangungu ngaio tētahi e whakamahere ana i te toronga atu ki tētahi whenua e whai whakaarohia ana he wāhi pakanga.

source: A search of the Internet for 'Hostile environment course' will probably provide the address of a local company.
reference: Mā te rapu i te ipurangi mo te "kōhi taiao kino" e kite ai i te wahi noho o tētahi kamupene ā-rohe.

source: A course will normally cover all the issues discussed here in far greater detail, usually with practical experience.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā ka aro tētahi akomanga ki te katoa o ngā take e kōrerotia ana ki konei me te taipitopito nui ake, i te nuinga o te wā ki te wheako ā-ringa.

source: A course will normally be from 2-5 days and will involve role play, a lot of first aid and sometimes weapons training.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā i waenga i te 2-5 ngā rā te roa o tētahi wānanga, ā, he whakatau kei roto, he nui te āwhina tuatahi, ā, i ētahi wā he whakangungu rākau.

source: Books and magazines dealing with wilderness survival are common, but publications dealing with war zones are few.
reference: He tini ngā pukapuka me ngā makahīni e kōrero ana mō te mōrehutanga ki te wao, heoi ehara i te mea he nui ngā putanga e pā ana ki ngā wāhi pakanga.

source: Voyagers planning sex reassignment surgery abroad must ensure they're carrying valid documents for the return trip.
reference: Ko ngā hunga haere e whakamahere ana i te poka panoni ira kei tāwāhi me whakarite e kawe ana rātou i ngā puka whai mana mō te hokinga mai.

source: The willingness of governments to issue passports with gender not stated (X) or documents updated to match a desired name and gender varies.
reference: Ko te hiahia o te kāwanatanga ki te tuku uruuruwhenua ki te kore e puakina te ira (X), ki ngā puka kua whakahoungia kia ōrite ki tētahi ingoa e hiahiatia ana, ira e hiahiatia ana rāne, ka rerekē tētahi i tētahi.

source: Willingness of foreign governments to honour these documents is just as widely variable.
reference: Ko te hiahia a te kāwana manene ki whakahōnore i ēnei pukapuka, he rerekē noa atu hoki.

source: Searches at security checkpoints have also become far more intrusive in the post-September 11, 2001 era.
reference: Kua manioro ake ngā whakatewhatewhanga i ngā tūngā a ngā kaiwhakahaumaru i te wā whai muri i te Hepetema 11, 2001.

source: Pre-operative transgender people should not expect to pass through the scanners with their privacy and dignity intact.
reference: Ko te hunga irawhiti i mua i te hāparapara me kaua e pōhēhē ka puta i ngā matawai me te ū tonu o te noho matatapu me te mana.

source: Rip currents are the returning flow from waves breaking off the beach, often at a reef or similar.
reference: Ko ngā ia kauere te hokinga mai o ngā ngaru e tūātea ana i te takutai moana, i te nuinga o te wā kei ngā ākau, kei ngā mea pērā rānei.

source: Due to the underwater topology the return flow is concentrated at a few deeper sections, and a fast current to deep water may form there.
reference: I te takotoranga o te papa ki raro i te wai e pīhangaiti ana te pari auraki ki ngā wāhanga hohonu, ā, kei hua ake hoki tētahi pari tere ki te wai hōhonu ki reira.

source: Most deaths happen as result of fatigue trying to swim back against the current, which may be impossible.
reference: Ko te nuinga o ngā mate ka ara ake i te ngenge i te wā e āki ana kia kaukau atu ki te au, i te mea akuanei pea he unuhanga taniwha tēnā.

source: As soon as you get out of the current, swimming back is no more difficult than normally.
reference: Ka puta tonu atu koe i te ia, ko te kaukau kia hoki atu kāore e uaua ake i te kaukau i te nuinga o te wā.

source: Try aiming somewhere where you are not caught again or, depending on your skills and on whether you have been noticed, you might want to wait for rescue.
reference: Ngana ki te aro atu ki tētahi wāhi kāore koe e mau anō, i runga rānei i ō pukenga, i tō kitea atu rānei, me tatari pea koe kia whakarauoratia.

source: Re-entry shock comes on sooner than culture shock (there's less of a honeymoon phase), lasts longer, and can be more severe.
reference: Ko te mauri rere o te kuhu anō ka tere ake te pā mai i tā te mauri rere ahurea (he iti iho te wā hanimuni), ka roa ake, ā, ka kino ake hoki pea.

source: Travellers who had an easy time adjusting to the new culture sometimes have a particularly hard time readjusting to their native culture.
reference: Ko te hunga hāereere he māmā te urutau ki te ahurea hou, i ētahi wā he uaua tā rātou urutau anō ki tō rātou ahurea taketake.

source: When returning home after living abroad, you've adapted to the new culture and lost some of your habits from your home culture.
reference: Ka hoki mai ana koe ki te wā kāinga, kua whai ahurea hou koe, ā, kua ngaro i a koe ētahi o ngā tikanga o te kāinga.

source: When you went abroad at first, people were probably patient and understanding, knowing that travellers in a new country need to adapt.
reference: I tō haerenga tuatahitanga ki tāwāhi, i te mānawanawa, i te āroharoha ngā tangata, i runga i te mōhio me urutau ngā tūruhi ki tētahi whenua hou.

source: People may not anticipate that patience and understanding are also necessary for travellers returning home.
reference: E kore pea ngā tāngata e whai whakaaro ake ko te manawa rau, ko te māramatanga he mea waiwai hoki mō te hunga hāereere e hoki ana ki te kāinga.

source: The pyramid sound and light show is one of the most interesting things in the area for kids.
reference: Ko te tangi oro me te whakaaturanga tīramarama tētahi o ngā mea hirahira katoa mō ngā tamariki.

source: You can see the pyramids in the dark and you can see them in silence before the show begins.
reference: Ka kitea ngā koeko ki te pōuri, ā, ka kitea i roto i te ngū i mua i te tīmatanga o te whakaaturanga.

source: Usually you always here the sound of tourists and vendors. The story of the sound and light is just like a story book.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā ka rongo i te oro o ngā tūruhi me ngā kaihoko. Ko ngā paki e pā ana ki te oro me te rama he pēnei i tētahi puka paki.

source: The Sphinx is set as the backdrop and the narrator of a long story.
reference: Ko te Sphnix ka noho hei tuarongo me te kaikōrero o tētahi paki tino roa.

source: The scenes are displayed on the pyramids and the different pyramids are lit up.
reference: Kei te whakaaturia ngā mata ki ngā koeko, ā, kei te tūramatia ngā tini koeko.

source: South Shetland Islands, discovered in 1819, are claimed by several nations and have the most bases, with sixteen active in 2020.
reference: Ko ngā Moutere o Shetland ki te Tonga, he moutere i kita i te tau 1819, e taunahatia ana e te nui ngā iwi o te ao, e 16 ngā moutere e rikarika ana.

source: The archipelago lies 120 km north of the Peninsula. The largest is King George Island with the settlement of Villa Las Estrellas.
reference: Kei te noho te huinga motu te 120 km ki te raki o te Raenga Kūiti. Ko te mea nui rawa ko King George Island me tōna kāinga o Villa Las Estrellas.

source: Others include Livingston Island, and Deception where the flooded caldera of a still-active volcano provides a spectacular natural harbour.
reference: Ko ētahi atu ko Livingston Island, me Deception kei reira tētahi whanga māori mīharo ki roto i te tawhā waipuke o tētahi rangitoto e oi tonu ana.

source: Ellsworth Land is the region south of the Peninsula, bounded by the Bellingshausen Sea.
reference: Ko Ellsworth Land te takiwā ki te tonga o te raenga kūiti, e paengia ana e te Bellingshausen Sea.

source: The mountains of the Peninsula here merge into the plateau, then re-emerge to form the 360 km chain of the Ellsworth Mountains, bisected by the Minnesota Glacier.
reference: Ko ngā pae maunga o konei ka whai wāhi atu ki te mānia, katahi ka rewa ake anō ki te hanga i te 360 km hononga o ngā Māunga Ellsworth, e weheruangia ana e te awa kōpaka o Minnesota.

source: The northern part or Sentinel Range has Antarctica's highest mountains, the Vinson Massif, peaking at 4892 m Mount Vinson.
reference: Kei te wāhanga raki, kei te Sentinel Range rānei ngā maunga teitei o Te Kōpakatanga ki te Tonga, arā ko te Vinson Massif, ko tōna tihi nui ko Mount Vinson ki te 4892 m.

source: In remote locations, without cell phone coverage, a satellite phone may be your only option.
reference: Ki ngā wāhi mohoao, karekau te pānga waea pūkoro, ko tētahi waea āmiorangi pea tō kōwhiringa kotahi.

source: A satellite phone is not generally a replacement for a mobile phone, as you have to be outdoors with clear line of sight to the satellite to make a phone call.
reference: Ehara te waea āmiorangi i te tino whakakapinga mō tētahi waea pūkoro, nā te mea me noho ki waho i te whare me te tirohanga wātea atu ki te āmiorangi kia pato i tētahi waeatanga atu.

source: The service is frequently used by shipping, including pleasure craft, as well as expeditions who have remote data and voice needs.
reference: Kei te whakamahia auautia te ratonga e te kaipuke, tae atu hoki ki ngā waka rēhia, waihoki ko ngā haerenga he hiahia ō rātou mō te raraunga me te reo mamao.

source: Your local telephone service provider should be able to give more information about connecting to this service.
reference: Me āhei tō kaituku ratonga waea ā-rohe te hoatu i ētahi atu mōhiohio mō te tūhono ki tēnei ratonga.

source: An increasingly more popular option for those planning a gap-year is to travel and learn.
reference: Ko tētahi kōwhiringa e piki haere ana te rorotu mō rātou e whakamahere ana i tētahi tau-waenga ko te hāereere me te ako.

source: This is especially popular with school leavers, allowing them to take a year out before university, without compromising their education.
reference: He pai rawa atu tēnei ki ngā ika ā-whiro, e tukuna ana rātou kia whakatā mō te kotahi tau i runga i te mōhio ka kore e pā mai he raru ki ā rātou e whai tohu ana.

source: In many cases, enrolling on a gap-year course abroad can actually improve your chances of moving into higher education back in your home country.
reference: He nui ngā wā, ko te uru atu ki tētahi akoranga tau-waenga ki tāwāhi ka mātua whakapakari ake i te tupono ka neke atu koe ki te mātauranga teitei ake ki tō whenua ūkaipō.

source: Typically there will be a tuition fee to enroll in these educational programs.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā he utu kura kia whakauru ki ēnei kaupapa ako.

source: Finland is a great boating destination. The "Land of a thousand lakes" has thousands of islands too, in the lakes and in the coastal archipelagos.
reference: He wāhi pai a Whinarana mō te taunga waka. Kei "Te Whenua o ngā rotomano", ngā motu manomano hoki, ki ngā roto me ngā huinga motu i te takutai.

source: In the archipelagos and lakes you do not necessarily need a yacht.
reference: Mō te āhua ki ngā moutere me ngā roto, hei aha noa iho te hērā.

source: Although the coastal archipelagos and the biggest lakes are indeed big enough for any yacht, smaller boats or even a kayak offer a different experience.
reference: Ahakoa ka rawaka pū te rahi o ngā huinga motu taha ki te whenua, me ngā roto rawhi rawa mō tētahi iata, mā ngā poti paku iho, mā te kōreti rānei ka puta he wheako rerekē.

source: Boating is a national pastime in Finland, with a boat to every seven or eight people.
reference: He mahi pārekareka te eke kaipuke ki roto o Finland, ko te kaipuka kotahi ki 7 tāngata, 8 tāngata rānei.

source: This is matched by Norway, Sweden and New Zealand, but otherwise quite unique (e.g. in the Netherlands the figure is one to forty).
reference: I rite nei tēnei ki Nōwei, ki Huitene, ki Aotearoa hoki, engari tonu hoki he tino ahurei (hei tauira, ki te whenua Hōrana he tahi ki te whā tekau te nama).

source: Most of the distinct Baltic Cruises feature an extended stay in St. Petersburg, Russia.
reference: Kei te nuinga o ngā Baltic Cruises tētahi noho āhua roa ki St. Petersburg, ki Rūhia.

source: This means you can visit the historic city for a couple of full days while returning and sleeping on the ship at night.
reference: Ko te tikanga ka āhei mai ki tēnei whenua rirerire mō ngā rā e rua, ka hoki mai ki te kaipuke moe ai i ngā pō.

source: If you only go ashore using shipboard excursions you will not need a separate visa (as of 2009).
reference: Mēnā koe ka pae ki uta mā runga kaipike, kaore noa he take o te pane uruwhenua (atu i te tau 2009).

source: Some cruises feature Berlin, Germany in the brochures. As you can see from the map above Berlin is no where near the sea and a visit to the city is not included in the price of the cruise.
reference: Kei ngā puka o ētahi rerenga moana ko Berlin ki Tiamani. Ka kitea koe ki te mahere ki runga kāore i te paku tata a Berlin ki te moana, ā, ko te toronga ki te tāone nui kāore i roto i te utu o te rerenga.

source: Travelling by plane can be a scary experience for people of all ages and backgrounds, particularly if they've not flown before or have experienced a traumatic event.
reference: Ka wehi te tangata ahakoa te pakeke ahakoa hoki nō hea ki te haerere mā te rererangi, ko te nuinga o te wā me kaore anō ia i rere, kua kino rānei tētahi wheakoranga.

source: It is not something to be ashamed of: it is no different from the personal fears and dislikes of other things that very many people have.
reference: Kāore he take kia whakamā: ehara i te rerekē i ngā mataku me ngā hae whaiaro ki ētahi atu mea kei te tokomaha.

source: For some, understanding something about how aircraft work and what happens during a flight may help to overcome a fear which is based on the unknown or on not being in control.
reference: Ki ētahi, mā te mārama ki ētahi āhuatanga o te mahi a ngā waka rererangi, me ngā mahi i te wā o te rerenga, he mea tautoko pea hei patu i te mataku e noho ana ki ngā āhuatanga kāore i te mōhiotia, o te noho kore mana whakahaere rānei.

source: Courier companies are well paid for delivering things quickly. Frequently, time is very important with business documents, merchandise or spare parts for an urgent repair.
reference: E utua paitia ana ngā kamupene koria mō te kawe tere i ngā mea. He nui ngā wā, he hirahira te wā mō te taha ki ngā pukapuka kamupene, ki ngā taputapu, ki ngā wāhanga mō tētahi whakatikanga whitawhita.

source: On some routes, the larger companies have their own planes, but for other routes and smaller firms there was a problem.
reference: I ētahi ara, he rererangi tā tēnā kamupene, tā tēnā kamupene, engari mō ētahi atu rā, i pā mai ētahi raruraru.

source: If they sent things by air freight, on some routes it may have taken days to get through unloading and customs.
reference: Mēnā rātou i tono i ngā mea mā runga, i ētahi ara ka ruarua pea ngā rā e uru mai ai i te wāhi tangotango me te whakatō.

source: The only way to get it through faster was to send it as checked luggage. Airline regulations will not allow them to send luggage without a passenger, which is where you come in.
reference: E tere ake ai, me tuku ki te wāhi tuku pēke. Ko ngā ture a te taunga rererangi, ka kore e tukuna ngā pēke ki te kore tētahi tangata e rere, ko koe tēnā.

source: The obvious way of flying in first or business class is to fork out a thick wad of money for the privilege (or, better yet, get your company to do it for you).
reference: Ko te huarahi mārakerake te kite mō te rere i ngā tūru rangatira i ngā tūru mahi rānei he hoatu i tētahi moni mātotoru ki te hunga whairawa ( ka pai ake mēnā ka taea e koe tō kamupene te mahi māu).

source: However, this does not come cheap: as rough rules of thumb, you can expect to pay up to four times the normal economy fare for business, and eleven times for first class!
reference: Heoi anō, ehara i te mea he iti te utu o tēnei: hei tohu āwhiwhi, ka utu pea koe i te whā whakareatanga o te utu iti te utu noa mō te pakihi, me te tekau mā tahi whakareanga mō te taumata tuatahi!

source: Generally speaking, there is no point in even looking for discounts for business or first-class seats on direct flights from A to B.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā, kāore he paku take o te rapu noa i ngā hekenga utu mō ngā tūru pakihi, karaehe-tuatahi rānei ki ngā rerenga tōtika i A ki B.

source: Airlines know well that there is a certain core group of flyers who are willing to pay top dollar for the privilege of getting somewhere fast and in comfort, and charge accordingly.
reference: Kei te mōhio pai ngā umanga waka rererangi he rōpū matua o te hunga rere e pai ana ki te utu i te utu nui rawa kia tae tere atu, āhuru atu ki tētahi wāhi, ā, ka pērā te whakarite utu.

source: The capital of Moldova is Chişinău. The local language is Romanian, but Russian is widely used.
reference: Ko te tāone matua o Moldova ko Chisinau. Ko tō rātou reo ko te reo Romānia engari kei te kōrerotia whānuitia te reo Rūhia.

source: Moldova is a multi-ethnic republic that has suffered from ethnic conflict.
reference: Ko Moldova tētahi whenua tini mātāwaka kua pāngia e te raru mātāwaka.

source: In 1994, this conflict led to the creation of the self-proclaimed Transnistria Republic in eastern Moldova, which has its own government and currency but is not recognised by any UN member country.
reference: I te tau 1994, i ara ake tēnei pakanga ki te hanganga o te Transnistria Republic ki te rāwhiti o Moldova nā rātou anō i whakapuaki, kei a rātou tō rātou ake kāwanatanga, pūnaha moni anō hoki, engari kāore i te whakamanatia e tētahi atu whenua he mema nō te Kotahitanga o Ngā Iwi o te Ao.

source: Economic links have been re-established between these two parts of Moldova despite the failure in political negotiations.
reference: Kua whakatūria anō ngā hononga ohaoha i waenganui i ēnei wāhanga e rua o Moldova ahakoa te hinganga ki ngā whakaritenga tōrangapū.

source: The major religion in Moldova is Orthodox Christian.
reference: Ko te hāhi matua ki Moldova ko te Karaitiana Tūturu.

source: İzmir is the third largest city in Turkey with a population of around 3.7 million, the second biggest port after Istanbul, and a very good transport hub.
reference: Ko Izmir te tāone nui he tuatoru tōna rahi ki Turkey me te taupori o te tata ki te 3.7 miriona, te xxx tuarua te rahi whai muri i Istanbul, me tētahi poka pū ikiiki tino pai.

source: Once the ancient city of Smyrna, it is now a modern, developed, and busy commercial center, set around a huge bay and surrounded by mountains.
reference: I tētahi wā te taone ukiuki nei o Smyrna, i tēnei wā he hou, he whanaketanga me tētahi pokapū e pōkea ana ki te mahi, ā, kei te takiwa o tētahi koko nui me ngā māunga e karapoti nei.

source: The broad boulevards, glass-fronted buildings and modern shopping centers are dotted with traditional red-tiled roofs, the 18th century market, and old mosques and churches, although the city has an atmosphere more of Mediterranean Europe than traditional Turkey.
reference: Ko ngā huarahi whānui, ko ngā whare he karāhe te pakitara o mua, me ngā pokapū hokohoko hou e kōtingotingo ana i waenga i ngā tuanui papauku-whero, te mākete nō te rautau 18, me ngā mosque me ngā whare karakia tawhito, ahakoa kei te tāone nui te wairua ka nui ake te Mediterranean Europe i te Tākei o mua.

source: The village of Haldarsvík offer views of the nearby island Eysturoy and has an unusual octagonal church.
reference: Ko tā te kāinga o Haldarsvik he tirohanga ki te motu tata o Esturoy, ā, he karakia tapawaru rerekē ki reira.

source: In the churchyard, there are interesting marble sculptures of doves over some tombs.
reference: I roto i te whare karakia, he whakapakoko kukupa māpere nei kei runga i ētahi urupā.

source: It's worth half an hour to stroll about the intriguing village.
reference: Hāwhe haora tō karore hāere i tēnei kāinga, he mea whakamiharo.

source: To the north and within easy reach is the romantic and fascinating town of Sintra and which was made famous to foreigners after a glowing account of its splendours recorded by Lord Byron.
reference: Ki te raki, ā, he māmā te tae atu ki reira, ko te tāone whakaingoingo, manawarū o Sintra, ā, i rongonui ai ki te hunga rāwaho i muri i te kōrero kauanuanu mō āna ātanga nā Lord Byron i tuhi.

source: Scotturb Bus 403 travels regularly to Sintra, stopping at Cabo da Roca.
reference: He rite tonu te haere a te Pahi Scotturb 403 ki Sintra, me te tau hoki ki Cabo da Roca.

source: Also to the north visit the great Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima (Shrine), a place of worldwide famous Marian apparitions.
reference: Ki te raki hoki me toro i te Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima (Shrine) nui whakaharahara, he wāhi o ngā mariko Marion rongonui puta noa i te ao.

source: Please remember that you are essentially visiting a mass grave site, as well as a site that has an almost incalculable meaning to a significant portion of the world's population.
reference: Kia maumahara kei te mātua toro atu koe ki tētahi wāhi nehu nui rawa, tae atu hoki ki tētahi wāhi e whai ana i te māramatanga e kore e taea te tatau ki tētahi wāhanga nui o te taupori o te ao.

source: There are still many men and women alive who survived their time here, and many more who had loved ones who were murdered or worked to death there, Jews and non-Jews alike.
reference: He tini tonu ngā tāne me ngā wāhine i mōrehutia ki konei, ā, he tini ake i ngaro i a rātou ō rātou makau nā te patunga, nā te mahinga kia mate rānei, ngā Hūrae me ngā Hūrae kore hoki.

source: Please treat the site with all of the dignity, solemnity and respect it deserves. Do not make jokes about the Holocaust or Nazis.
reference: Tēnā koa whai whakaaro mai ki te mana, te ihi, te wehi o tēnei wāhi. Kaua e pukuhohe mō ngā mahi whakapoauau a ngā Nazi.

source: Do not deface the site by marking or scratching graffiti into structures.
reference: Kauka te wāhi e whakaanuanu mā te māka mā te raku i te karawhiti ki ngā hanganga.

source: Barcelona's official languages are Catalan and Spanish. About a half prefer to speak Catalan, a vast majority understands it, and virtually everyone knows Spanish.
reference: Ko ngā reo ōkawa o Barcelona ko Catalan me Pāniora. Tata ki te haurua ka pai ake ki te kōrero Catalan, ka māramatia e te tino nuinga, ā, tata ki te katoa e mōhio ana ki te reo Pāniora.

source: However, most signs are indicated only in Catalan because it is established by law as the first official language.
reference: Heoi anō, kei te tohua te nuinga o ngā tohu ki te reo Catalan anake nā te mea kua whakaūngia e te ture koirā te reo ōkawa tuatahi.

source: Yet, Spanish is also widely used in public transport and other facilities.
reference: Heoi, E māhorahora ana te kōrerotia o te reo Pāniora ki ngā wāhi tūmatanui me ērā momo wāhi.

source: Regular announcements in the Metro are made only in Catalan, but unplanned disruptions are announced by an automated system in a wide variety of languages including Spanish, English, French, Arabic and Japanese.
reference: Ko ngā pānui auau i te Metro e mahi noa ana ki Catalan, heoi ko ngā putanga kōrero raruraru kore perēne nā te pūnaha mīhini auau i roto i ngā tini reo pēnei i te Paniora, te Ingarihi, te Wīwi, te Araby me te Hapanihi.

source: Parisians have a reputation for being egocentric, rude and arrogant.
reference: E mōhiotia ana te iwi Parisia he iwi whakahīhī, he kuare, he whakamenemene.

source: While this is often only an inaccurate stereotype, the best way to get along in Paris still is to be on your best behavior, acting like someone who is "bien élevé" (well brought up). It will make getting about considerably easier.
reference: Ahakoa he nui ngā wā he whakaaro horapa hē tēnei, ko te ara pai rawa kia pai te haere ki Parī kia pai rawa tō whanonga, me pērā i tētahi tangata he "bien eleve" (kua pai te whakatipu). Mā korā e māmā ake ai te neke haere.

source: Parisians' abrupt exteriors will rapidly evaporate if you display some basic courtesies.
reference: Ko te āhua māro o te hunga nō Parī ka tere ngaro atu mēnā ka whakaatu koe i ētahi atawhaitanga māmā.

source: The Plitvice Lakes national park is heavily forested, mainly with beech, spruce, and fir trees, and features a mixture of Alpine and Mediterranean vegetation.
reference: He nui te ngahere ki te Plitvice Lakes national park, ko te nuinga he rākau beech, he spruce, he fir, ā, he huinga o ngā otaota Alpine, Mediterranean hoki ki reira.

source: It has a notably wide variety of plant communities, due to its range of microclimates, differing soils and varying levels of altitude.
reference: He tino whānui te rerenga o ngā hapori tipu, i runga i ōna āhuarangi-whāiti, ōna one rerekē, me ngā taumata teitei rerekē.

source: The area is also home to an extremely wide variety of animal and bird species.
reference: He kāinga hoki tēnei rohe ki te tino whānuitanga rawa o te rerenga o ngā momo kararehe, manu hoki.

source: Rare fauna such as the European brown bear, wolf, eagle, owl, lynx, wild cat and capercaillie can be found there, along with many more common species
reference: Ko ngā kararehe ongeonge pērā i te pia parāone o Ūropi, i te wuruhi, i te īkara, i te koukou, i te riniki, i te ngeru mohoao, i te capercaillie ka kitea ki reira, me ngā tini momo anō hoki.

source: While visiting the monasteries, women are required to wear skirts covering the knees and have their shoulders covered, too.
reference: I te wā e toro ana ki ngā whare monaki, e hiahiatia ana te wahine ki te mau panekoti e uwhi ana i ngā turi, ā, kei te uwhia hoki ō rātou pakihiwi.

source: Most of the monasteries do provide wraps for women who come unprepared, but if you bring your own, especially one with bright colors, you'll get a smile from the monk or nun at the entrance.
reference: Ko te nuinga o ngā whare karakia ka tuku i ngā takai mā ngā wāhine ka taetae atu me te kore i whakarite, engari ki te kawe atu koe i tō ake takai, inarā ko tētahi he kitakita ngā tae ka menemene mai te manake, te none rānei ki te tomokanga.

source: Along the same line, men are required to wear trousers covering the knees.
reference: Ko taua āhua anō rā, me mau tarau ngā tāne kia kore ai e kitea ngā pona.

source: This too can be borrowed from the stock at the entrance but that clothing isn't washed after every user so you may not feel comfortable wearing these skirts. One size fits all for men!
reference: Ka taea hoki tēnei te mino mai i ngā rawa ki te tomokanga engari kāore ērā kākahu i te horoia whai muri i ia kaimahi nō reira e kore pea koe e pai ki te mau i ēnei panekoti. E pai ana te rahi kotahi ki ngā tāne katoa!

source: Majorcan cuisine, like that of similar zones in the Mediterranean, is based on bread, vegetables and meat (specially pork), and uses olive oil throughout.
reference: Ko ngā kai Majorcan, e rite ana ki ētahi wāhi ki Metatareina, he paraoa te kai matua, he huawhenua, he mīti (kōia tonu ko te poaka), me te whakamahinga o te hinu oriwa hoki.

source: A simple popular dinner, especially during the summer, is the Pa amb Oli: Bread with olive oil, tomato, and any available condiments such as cheese, tunafish, etc.
reference: Ko tētahi tina rorotu, inarā i te wā o te raumati, ko te Pa amb Oli: Te parāoa ki te hinu ōriwa, te tomato, me ngā kīnaki e wātea ana pērā i te tīhi, te ika, aha atu.

source: All nouns, alongside the word Sie for you, always begin with a capital letter, even in the middle of a sentence.
reference: Ko ngā kupu ingoa katoa, tae atu ki te kupu Sie mōu, ka tīmata ki te pūmatua, ahakoa kei waenganui i te rerenga.

source: This is an important way to distinguish between some verbs and objects.
reference: He ara hira tēnei hei whakawehe i waenga i ētahi kupu mahi me ngā aronga.

source: It also arguably makes reading easier, though writing is somewhat complicated by the need to find out whether a verb or adjective is used in a substantivized form.
reference: Ka taea hoki te kī kei te whakamāmā ia i te pānui, ahakoa kei te whakatāwhiwhitia te tuhituhi e te hiahia kia whai mōhio mēnā rānei kei te whakamahia tētahi kupu mahi, kupu whakaahua rānei ki tētahi āhua whakakupuingoa.

source: Pronunciation is relatively easy in Italian since most words are pronounced exactly how they are written
reference: He māmā noa te whakahua i te reo Itāria nō te mea e ōrite ana te whakahua o te nuinga o ngā kupu ki te takotoranga tuhi.

source: The main letters to watch out for are c and g, since their pronunciation varies based on the following vowel.
reference: Ko ngā reta matua me mataara ko te c me te g, i te mea e rerekē ai te whakahuatanga e ai ki te oropuare e whai ake ana.

source: Also, make sure to pronounce r and rr differently: caro means dear, whereas carro means chariot.
reference: Me te mea hoki, me rerekē te whakahuatanga o te r hei me te rr: ko te caro he tia, ā, ko te carro he hāriata.

source: Persian has a relatively easy and mostly regular grammar.
reference: Kei te reo Pāhia tētahi wete reo āhua māmā, āhua riterite.

source: Therefore, reading this grammar primer would help you learn much about Persian grammar and understand phrases better.
reference: Nā reira, mā te pānui i ēnei pukapuka wetereo e mārama ake ai koe ki ngā tikanga wetereo ki Persia, me te whakatakotoranga o ngā rerenga kōrero.

source: Needless to say, if you know a Romance language, it will be easier for you to learn Portuguese.
reference: E pūrangiaho te kitea, mēnā ka mōhio koe ki tētahi reo mate kanehe,, ka māmā ake tāu ako i te reo Potukara.

source: However, people who know a little Spanish may hastily conclude that Portuguese is close enough that it need not be studied separately.
reference: Heoi anō, ko te hunga e mōhio ana ki te paku reo Pāniora ka tere whakatau pea ka pērā te ōrite o te reo Potukara kia kaua e ako wehe i taua reo.

source: Pre-modern observatories are usually obsolete today, and remain as museums, or sites of education.
reference: Ko te nuinga o ngā whare mātai ātea tawhito e korehāhā ana i tēnei rā, ā, ka noho tohu hei whare taonga, hei wāhi whakaako rānei.

source: As light pollution in their heyday was not the kind of problem it is today, they are usually located in cities or at campuses, easier to reach than those built in modern times.
reference: I te mea mea, kāore e pērā rawa ana te kino o te hauārangi kino i te wā i a rātou, ka kitea i ngā tāone kei taua takiwā me ngā kuratini, he māmā ake te toro atu i ngā mea i hangaia ō mohoa nei.

source: Most modern research telescopes are enormous facilities in remote areas with favorable atmospheric conditions.
reference: Ko te nuinga o ngā karu whātata rangahau hou he hanganga rahi rawa ki ngā wāhi kōkēi me ngā āhuatanga kōhauhau pai.

source: Cherry blossom viewing, known as hanami, has been a part of Japanese culture since the 8th century.
reference: Ko te hanami he tirotiro ki ngā pūāwai tiere, ā, kua pēnei te ahurea Hapanihi mai i te rautau tuawaru.

source: The concept came from China where plum blossoms were the flower of choice.
reference: I ahu mai te whakaaro nei i Haina ko reira whiriwhiri ai te putiputi paramu hei kōwhiringa pua.

source: In Japan, the first cherry blossom parties were hosted by the emperor only for himself and other members of the aristocracy around the Imperial Court.
reference: Ki Hapāni, ko te pua puaina tuatahi nā te emepara i whakatū mōna anake, me ōna apataki o te kāhui ariki me te kōti teitei.

source: Plants look their best when in a natural environment, so resist the temptation to remove even "just one" specimen.
reference: He pai ake te hanga o te rākau i roto i tōna ake taiao, nōreira me kauaka e kato i "tētahi" paku mea mai i tana wāhi.

source: If visiting a formally arranged garden, collecting "specimens" is also going to get you ejected, without discussion.
reference: Ki te toro atu koe ki tētahi māra kua āta whakamaheretia, mā te kohi "pota" ka whakaputaina koe, karekau te kōrerorero.

source: Singapore is generally an extremely safe place to be and very easy to navigate, and you can buy almost anything after arriving.
reference: I te nuinga o te wā he wāhi tīno haumrau a Singapore me te ngāwari hoki ki te hautū haere, e taea ana e koe te nuinga o ngā mea te hoko i muri i te taenga atu.

source: But being placed in the "high tropics" just a few degrees north of equator you will need to deal with both heat (always) and strong sun (when the sky is clear, more rarely).
reference: Ēngari nā te noho ki ngā "takiwa tino pārū" ētahi tīkiri noa ki te raki o te weheruatanga o te ao ka pāngia koe e te wera (i ngā wā katoa) me te kaha o te rā (i te wā he rangi paki, he iti ake ngā wā).

source: There are also a few buses going north to Hebron, the traditional burial place of the Biblical patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and their wives.
reference: Tērā hoki ētahi pahi e haere whakateraki ana ki Hebron, te wāhi urupā taketake o te pā whakawairua o Aperehama nō te Paipera, o Ihaka, o Hāpoka me ā rātou wāhine.

source: Check that the bus you are thinking of taking goes into Hebron and not just to the nearby Jewish settlement of Kiryat Arba.
reference: Me whakatūturu ko te pahi e whakaaro ana koe kia eke ka haere ki Hebron, kaua ki te kāinga Hūrae tata o Kiryat Arba noa iho.

source: Inland waterways can be a good theme to base a holiday around.
reference: He pai ngā arawai o uta hei kaupapa ārahi i ō haerenga.

source: For example visiting castles in the Loire Valley, the Rhine valley or taking a cruise to interesting cites on the Danube or boating along the Erie Canal.
reference: Hei tauira ko te toro atu ki ngā whare kīngi ki te Loire Valley, ki te Rhine valley, te eke rānei ki tētahi rerenga waka ki ngā tāone nui manawa reka ki te Danube, te eke waka rānei ki te Erie Canal.

source: They also define routes for popular hiking and cycling trails.
reference: Ka tautuhia hoki ngā ara mō te hīkoi rongonui me ngā ara paihikara.

source: Christmas is one of the most important holidays of Christianity, and is celebrated as the birthday of Jesus.
reference: Ko te Kirihimete ko tētahi o ngā hararei whakahirahira rawa o te Karaitiana, ā, ka whakanuia hei rāwhānau o Ihu.

source: Many of the traditions surrounding the holiday have been adopted also by non-believers in Christian countries and non-Christians around the world.
reference: He nui tonu ngā tīkanga mō te hararei kua kawea hoki e ngā tāngata-kore-whakapono i ngā whenua Karaitiana me te hunga ehara i te Karaitiana huri noa i te ao.

source: There's a tradition to pass the Easter night awake at some exposed point to see the sunrise.
reference: Arā tētahi tikanga kia noho oho mō te roanga o te pō Aranga ki tētahi wāhi mārakerake hei kite i te aranga mai o te rā.

source: There are of course Christian theological explanations for this tradition, but it may well be a pre-Christian Spring and Fertility ritual.
reference: He whakamāramatanga ā-hāhi Karaitiana tonu mō tēnei tikanga, engari tonu he ritenga pea nō mua i te wā Karaitiana mō te Kōanga me te Matahua.

source: More traditional churches often hold an Easter Vigil on Saturday night during the Easter weekend, with the congregations often breaking into celebration at the stroke of midnight to celebrate Christ's resurrection.
reference: Ko ngā whare karakia tūturu ake o mua ka mau tonu ki te Easter Vigil i te pō o te Hatarei i te wā o te mutunga wiki Aranga, ā, ko ngā minenga ka puta ki te whakanuitanga i te wā pū tonu o te tūruawēpō kia whakanui i te aranga mai o Ihu.

source: All animals that originally arrived in the islands came here either by swimming, flying or floating.
reference: Ko ngā kararehe katoa i tae tūturu mai ki ngā motu i tae mai ki konei nā te kaukau, te rere hau, te rere wai rānei.

source: Due to the long distance from the continent mammals were unable to make the journey making the giant tortoise the primary grazing animal in the Galapagos.
reference: E ai ki te roanga mai i te paparahi kāore ngā whāngote i āhei te haere nō reira ko te giant tortoise te kararehe kai otaota matua ki ngā Galapagos.

source: Since the arrival of man to the Galapagos, many mammals have been introduced including goats, horses, cows, rats, cats and dogs.
reference: Mai anō i te taenga mai o te tangata ki Galapagos, kua tomokia atu ngā tini kararehe pēnei i ngā koti, ngā hoiho, kau, kiore, ngeru me ngā kurī.

source: If you visit the Arctic or Antarctic areas in the winter you will experience the polar night, which means that the sun doesn't rise above the horizon.
reference: Mēnā koe ka haere ki te Arctic, ki te Antarctic rānei i ngā wā o te takurua, ka kite koa i te pō poara, tōna tikanga hoki kāore te rā e awatea mai i te paerangi.

source: This offers a good opportunity to see the Aurora borealis, as the sky will be dark more or less around the clock.
reference: He hoatutanga pai tēnei kia kite ai i ngā Aurora Borealis, i te mea ka pōuri te rangi mō te roanga o te huringa o te karaka.

source: As the areas are sparsely populated, and light pollution therefore often not a problem, you will also be able to enjoy the stars.
reference: I te mea kua itiiti nei te rahinga tāngata, kāore he tino raruraru o te parakino-raiti, ā, ka manawareka anō hoki koe ki ngā whetū.

source: Japanese work culture is more hierarchical and formal that what Westerners may be used to.
reference: He nui ake te ara ā-kapa, te ōkawa hoki o te ahurea mahi o Hapani i tā te hunga Uru e waia ana.

source: Suits are standard business attire, and coworkers call each other by their family names or by job titles.
reference: He māori noa te mau hūtu mō te pakihi, ā, ka karangatia ngā kaimahi e rātou ake ki ō rātou ingoa whānau ki ō rātou taitara mahi hoki rānei.

source: Workplace harmony is crucial, emphasizing group effort rather than praising individual accomplishments.
reference: He mea waiwai te reretahi ki te wāhi mahi, me te aro atu ki te mahi a te rōpū kaua ko te mihi ki ngā ekenga takitahi.

source: Workers must often get their superiors' approval for any decisions they make, and are expected to obey their superiors' instructions without question.
reference: He nui ngā wā me whai whakaaetanga ngā kaimahi i ō rātou rangatira mō ngā whakatau katoa, ā, e hiahiatia ana ka whai rātou i ngā tohutohu o ō rātou rangatira horekau te pātai atu.

source: "Kei a tātou ināianei ngā kiore e 4-marama te pakeke kāore i te pāngia e te mate huka, ahakoa i mua i pāngia e te mate huka," tana kōrero tāpiri.
reference: "We now have 4-month-old mice that are non-diabetic that used to be diabetic," he added.

source: Ko ngā whakatūpato a Tākuta Ehud Ur, Ahorangi mō te Rongoā ki Te Whare Wānanga o Dalhousie, kei Halifax, Nova Scotia, ko te heamana hoki mō ngā mahi whakamātau rongoā, pūtaiao hoki ki te Canadian Diabetes Association, kei te mata tonu ngā mahi rangahau.
reference: Dr. Ehud Ur, professor of medicine at Dalhousie University in Halifax, Nova Scotia and chair of the clinical and scientific division of the Canadian Diabetes Association cautioned that the research is still in its early days.

source: Pēnei i ētahi atu tōhunga, kei te ruarua kē ōna mahara e pā ana ki te whakaora i te mate huka, me te mōhio iho kāore ēnei kōrero e hāngai ana ki te hunga kua pāngia kētia ki te mate huka momo 1.
reference: Like some other experts, he is skeptical about whether diabetes can be cured, noting that these findings have no relevance to people who already have Type 1 diabetes.

source: I te Mane, i whakapuaki tūmatanuitia e ara Danius, te hekeretari ū o te Nobel Committee for Literature ki te Swedish Academy, i tētahi hōtaka reo irirangi ki Sveriges Radio ki Huitene ko te komiti, kāore i te āhei ki te whakapā tōtika atu ki a Bob Dylan mō tāna toa i te 2016 Nobel Prize in Literature, kua whakarere i āna mahi ki te pā atu ki a ia.
reference: On Monday, Sara Danius, permanent secretary of the Nobel Committee for Literature at the Swedish Academy, publicly announced during a radio program on Sveriges Radio in Sweden the committee, unable to reach Bob Dylan directly about winning the 2016 Nobel Prize in Literature, had abandoned its efforts to reach him.

source: Nā Danius i kī, "Ināiatonunei kāore mātou i te paku aha. Kua waea atu au, kua tuku īmēra anō hoki ki tōna hoa mahi ngātahi tata rawa, ā, kua tae mai he whakahoki tino hoahoa. Mō tēnei wā, kua nui tērā."
reference: Danius said, "Right now we are doing nothing. I have called and sent emails to his closest collaborator and received very friendly replies. For now, that is certainly enough."

source: I mua atu, nā te CEO o Ring, nā Jamie Siminoff i kī, i tīmata te kamupene i te wā kāore tāna pere kuaha i te rangona mai i tana toa ki tōna karāti.
reference: Previously, Ring's CEO, Jamie Siminoff, remarked the company started when his doorbell wasn't audible from his shop in his garage.

source: I hangaia mai e ia he pere kuaha ahokore.
reference: He built a WiFi door bell, he said.

source: I kī a Siminoff i piki ake ngā hokonga whai muri i tana putanga i te hōtaka Shark Tank i te tau 2013 i te wā kāore te paewhiri o te hōtaka i whakaae kia hoatu moni mō te timatanga o te pākihi.
reference: Siminoff said sales boosted after his 2013 appearance in a Shark Tank episode where the show panel declined funding the startup.

source: I te mutunga o te tau 2017, i kitea a Siminoff ki te hongere pouaka whakaata hoko, ki QVC.
reference: In late 2017, Siminoff appeared on shopping television channel QVC.

source: I whakatau hoki a Ring i tētahi take ture ki te kamupene whakahaumaru tātāwhāinga, ko te ADT Corporation.
reference: Ring also settled a lawsuit with competing security company, the ADT Corporation.

source: Ahakoa ko te āhua o tētahi rongoā āraimate e whakamātautauhia ana he āwhina pea i te mate mai i te Ebola, tae noa mai ki tēnei wā, karekau he rongoā kua pai te whakaatu kia whakamaimoa i te pokenga kua pā kē.
reference: While one experimental vaccine appears able to reduce Ebola mortality, up until now, no drugs have been clearly demonstrated suitable for treating existing infection.

source: Kotahi te pāhekoheko paturopi, te ZMapp, i whakaatu tuatahi i ētahi putanga papai i te mātai, heoi nā ngā rangahau ōkawa i whakaatu he iti ake ngā painga i tērā i pīrangitia mō te haukoti i te mate.
reference: One antibody cocktail, ZMapp, initially showed promise in the field, but formal studies indicated it had less benefit than sought in preventing death.

source: Ki te whakamātau PALM, ko Zmapp hei pae, arā ka whakamahia e ngā kaipūtaiao hei pūtake, ā, ka whakatairitea ngā maimoatanga hou e toru i tērā.
reference: In the PALM trial, ZMapp served as a control, meaning scientists used it as a baseline and compared the three other treatments to it.

source: Kei te tautoko a USA Gymnastics i te reta a te United States Olympic Committee, ā, kei te whakaae ki te hiahia waiwai o te whānau Olympic hei tuku i tētahi taiao haumaru mō te katoa o ā mātou kaipara.
reference: USA Gymnastics supports the United States Olympic Committee's letter and accepts the absolute need of the Olympic family to promote a safe environment for all of our athletes.

source: Kei te whakaae mātou ki te tauākī a te USOC ko ngā pānga o ā mātou kaioma, karapū hoki, tae atu ki tā rātou momo hākinakina, ka pai ake te tautoko i a rātou mā te koke whakamua ki te panoni whai tikanga ki roto tonu i tō tātou whakahaere, kaua ko te whakakore tiwhikete.
reference: We agree with the USOC's statement that the interests of our athletes and clubs, and their sport, may be better served by moving forward with meaningful change within our organization, rather than decertification.

source: Ka tautoko te takaporepore USA i tētahi tirohanga motuhake e rewa ake ai ngā āhutanga whakamanioro o ngā mahinga i kōrerotia māiangia e ngā mōrehu o Larry Nassar, i āhei pea te haere hara kore mō te wā roa, ā, ahakoa te tīnitanga, me mahi.
reference: USA Gymnastics supports an independent investigation that may shine light on how abuse of the proportion described so courageously by the survivors of Larry Nassar could have gone undetected for so long and embraces any necessary and appropriate changes.

source: He ōrite nei tā USA Gymnastics me tā USOC whāinga - i runga i tēnā kia āta manaakitia te hākinakina takahurihuri kia taea ai e ngā kaiaka te whai i ā rātou whāinga i roto i tētahi taiao manaaki me te tiaki.
reference: USA Gymnastics and the USOC have the same goal — making the sport of gymnastics, and others, as safe as possible for athletes to follow their dreams in a safe, positive and empowered environment.

source: Mō te roanga o ngā tau 1960, i mahi a Brzezinski mā John F. Kennedy hei kaitohutohu, ā, katahi ko te tari o Lyndon B. Johnson.
reference: Throughout 1960s, Brzezinski worked for John F. Kennedy as his advisor and then the Lyndon B. Johnson administration.

source: I te wā o ngā kōwhiringa 1976 i tohutohu ia ki a Carter mō ngā kaupapa here ā-ao, ā, kātahi ia ka tū hei National Security Advisor (NSA) mai i te tau 1977 ki te tau 1981, ka whai i a Henare Kissinger.
reference: During the 1976 selections he advised Carter on foreign policy, then served as National Security Advisor (NSA) from 1977 to 1981, succeeding Henry Kissinger.

source: I tōna tūranga ki te NSA, i tautoko ia i a Carter i roto i ngā take huhua o te ao, pērā ki ngā take ki Camp David i te 1978, ngā mahinga ngātahi i waenga i a Amerika me Haina i te pito o ngā tau 1970, ngā uauatanga ki Iran i hua ai te raru nui ki reira i te tau 1979 me te urutomonga o te Soviet ki roto o Afghanistan i te tau 1979.
reference: As NSA, he assisted Carter in diplomatically handling world affairs, such as the Camp David Accords, 1978; normalizing US–China relations thought the late 1970s; the Iranian Revolution, which led to the Iran hostage crisis, 1979; and the Soviet invasion in Afghanistan, 1979.

source: Ko te kiriata, e whakaatu ana i a Ryan Gosling me Emma Stone, i tohua ki ngā wāhanga nui katoa.
reference: The movie, featuring Ryan Gosling and Emma Stone, received nominations in all major categories.

source: I tohua a Gosling rāua ko Stone hei Best Actor me Best Actress.
reference: Gosling and Stone received nominations for Best Actor and Actress respectively.

source: Ko ētahi ingoa kē atu kua whakaurungia ko te Pikitia Pai, te kaihautu, te kiriataata, te Hoahoa Tākahu, te Whakatika Kiriata, te Tohu Tuatahi, te Hanga Hoahoa, te Whakapai Oro, te Whakaranu Oro me te Kiriata Taketakenga.
reference: The other nominations include Best Picture, Director, Cinematography, Costume Design, Film-editing, Original Score, Production Design, Sound Editing, Sound Mixing and Original Screenplay.

source: E rua ngā waiata i te kiriata, Audition (The Fools Who Dream) me City of Stars, i whiwhi whakaaringa mō te waiata tūturu pai rawa. I whiwhi a Lionsgate studio i te 26 whakaaringa - he nui ake i tētahi atu taupuni mahi.
reference: Two songs from the movie, Audition (The Fools Who Dream) and City of Stars, received nominations for best original song. Lionsgate studio received 26 nominations — more than any other studio.

source: I te mutunga o te Rātapu, i roto i tētahi tauāki nā te Perehitene o Amerika nā Donald Trump i te heketeri o ngā perehi, i kī ai ka wehe ngā hōia Amerika i a Syria.
reference: Late on Sunday, the United States President Donald Trump, in a statement delivered via the press secretary, announced US troops would be leaving Syria.

source: I whakapuakina whai muri i te kōrero ā-waea a Trump ki te Perehetini nō Tākei a Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
reference: The announcement was made after Trump had a phone conversation with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

source: Ka whai mana hoki a Tākei mō te tiaki i ngā kaiwhawhai ISIS kua mauheretia, arā, e ai ki te puakitanga, kua karo ngā whenua Ūropi ki te whakahoki atu i a rātou.
reference: Turkey would also take over guarding captured ISIS fighters which, the statement said, European nations have refused to repatriate.

source: E kī ana tēnei i mau raukura ētahi mokoweri, he ariā e whakaponotia whānuitia ana, engari he kōrero e mau ana ka kore nei e kitea ki runga anga pōhatu, pērā ki te tae, me te tōna hanga ahu tengi nei.
reference: This not only confirms that at least some dinosaurs had feathers, a theory already widespread, but provides details fossils generally cannot, such as color and three-dimensional arrangement.

source: e kī nei ngā kaipūtaiao ko ngā huruhuru o tēnei kararehe he parauri o runga, ā, he kōmā, he ārani o raro.
reference: . Scientists say this animal's plumage was chestnut-brown on top with a pale or carotenoid-colored underside.

source: Mā tēnei kitenga e kite ai te māramatanga ki te kukuwhatanga o ngā raukura manu.
reference: The find also grants insight into the evolution of feathers in birds.

source: I te mea kāore ngā huruhuru tainahora i te whanake pai i te tuara, e kī ana ko te rachis, ēngari kāore he āhuatanga kē atu o te huruhuru - ko ngā niwha me ngā barbules - e kī ana ngā kairangahau kāore e kore ko te rachis tētahi mea i kukuwha ake i muri ake i ēnei atu āhuatanga.
reference: Because the dinosaur feathers do not have a well-developed shaft, called a rachis, but do have other features of feathers — barbs and barbules — the researchers inferred the rachis was likely a later evolutionary development that these other features.

source: E ai ki te hanganga o ngā huru kāore i whakamahia kia rere, engari kē mō te whakahaere paemahana, te whakaaturanga rānei. I marohi ngā kairangahau, ahakoa koinei te whiore o tētahi kūao mokonui, kei te kitea ki te tauira ngā huruhuru pakeke, kaua ko ngā huruhuru pīpī.
reference: The feathers' structure suggests that they were not used in flight but rather for temperature regulation or display. The researchers suggested that, even though this is the tail of a young dinosaur, the sample shows adult plumage and not a chick's down.

source: I marohitia e ngā kairangahau, ahakoa koinei te whiore o tētahi mokonui punua, kei te whakaaturia e te tauira ngā huruhuru o te pakeke, kaua ko te pīpī.
reference: The researchers suggested that, even though this is the tail of a young dinosaur, the sample shows adult plumage and not a chick's down.

source: I pahū mai tētahi pōmu waka ki te wharenui o ngā pirhimana ki Gaziantep Tākei i te ata o nanahi. Ka mate ngā pirihimana e rua, ka neke atu i te rua tekau anō i whara.
reference: A car bomb detonated at police headquarters in Gaziantep, Turkey yesterday morning killed two police officers and injured more than twenty other people.

source: I kīia e te tari o te kāwanam he tekau mā iwa o te hunga i whara he pirihimana.
reference: The governor's office said nineteen of the injured were police officers.

source: E whakapae ana ngā pirihimana ko te tangata tūkino he hōia Daesh (ISIL).
reference: Police said they suspect an alleged Daesh (ISIL) militant of responsibility for the attack.

source: I kite ai rātou e ōrite ana ngā mātāpono o te Rā ki ētahi atu whetū: Nā te whērikoriko o ngā whetū katoa o te kāhui whetū, ō rātou huringa hoki.
reference: They found the Sun operated on the same basic principles as other stars: The activity of all stars in the system was found to be driven by their luminosity, their rotation, and nothing else.

source: Kei te whakamahia tahitia te kanapā me te huringa hei whakatau i te tau Rossby a tētahi tau, ā, e hāngai ana tērā ki te rere wētoto.
reference: The luminosity and rotation are used together to determine a star's Rossby number, which is related to plasma flow.

source: Te iti iho o te nama Rossby, ka ngoikore ake te whetū mō te āhuatanga ki te hurihanga aukume.
reference: The smaller the Rossby number, the less active the star with respect to magnetic reversals.

source: I tana haerenga, he nui ngā wā i raru a Iwasaki.
reference: During his trip, Iwasaki ran into trouble on many occasions.

source: I pāhua ia e ngā kaiā moana, i tukia ia i Tipete e tētahi kurī kua pāngia e te reipi, i pakiha ia i te mārenatanga i Nepōra, ā, i mauheretia ki Īnia.
reference: He was robbed by pirates, attacked in Tibet by a rabid dog, escaped marriage in Nepal and was arrested in India.

source: Ko te paerewa 802.11n ka kōkiritia mā runga i te iarere 2.4Ghz me te 5.0Ghz
reference: The 802.11n standard operates on both the 2.4Ghz and 5.0Ghz frequencies.

source: Mā tēnei ka muri hototahi i te 802.11a, te 802.11b me te 802.11g, i runga i te āhuatanga e whai irirangi takirua ana te teihana.
reference: This will allow it to be backwards compatible with 802.11a, 802.11b and 802.11g, provided that the base station has dual radios.

source: Ko ngā tere o te 802.11n he tino tere ake i tērā o ngā mea o mua ko tā rātou putanga ariā nui rawa ko te 600Mbit/s.
reference: The speeds of 802.11n are substantially faster than that of its predecessors with a maximum theoretical throughput of 600Mbit/s.

source: Kāore a Duvall, kua mārenatia me ngā tamaiti pakeke e rua i waiho i tētahi whakaaro nui mō Miller, mō te take o tēnei kōrero.
reference: Duvall, who is married with two adult children, did not leave a big impression on Miller, to whom the story was related.

source: Ka pātaia, ko tā Miller, "he ngutu hohoro a Mike i ngā wā o te whakawātanga... i te whakatika au i a au anō, kāore au i rongo i a ia.
reference: When asked for comment, Miller said, "Mike talks a lot during the hearing...I was getting ready so I wasn't really hearing what he was saying."

source: "Ka ngana tātou kia haukoti i te putanga waro hāora-rua ki ia waeine o te GDP ki tētahi rahinga nui, i mua i te tau 2020 mai i te taumata o te tau 2005," te kī a Hu.
reference: "We will endeavour to cut carbon dioxide emissions per unit of GDP by a notable margin by 2020 from the 2005 level," Hu said.

source: Kāore ia i whakarite i ētahi nama mō ngā tapatapahi, e kī ana ka whakaritea i runga i te tāputa o te ohaoha o Haina.
reference: He did not set a figure for the cuts, saying they will be made based on China's economic output.

source: I akiaki a Hu i ngā whenua e whanake ana "kia āraia te huarahi o mua o te parakino i te tuatahi, me te whakapai i muri iho ."
reference: Hu encouraged developing countries "to avoid the old path of polluting first and cleaning up later."

source: Nāna i tāpiri "heoi anō, me kaua rātou e tonoa kia hiki i ngā kawenga kei tua atu i tā rātou wā whakawhanaketanga, haepapa, āheinga hoki."
reference: He added that "they should not, however, be asked to take on obligations that go beyond their development stage, responsibility and capabilities."

source: I tukuna e te Iraq Study Group tāna pūrongo i te 12.00 GMT i tēnei rā.
reference: The Iraq Study Group presented its report at 12.00 GMT today.

source: He whakatūpato, kāore tētahi e āhei te kī ake mā tētahi mahinga ahakoa te aha e whakatū i te whawhai whakahāweatanga, i ngā kinonga e tupu tonu ana, i te tutūnga o te pūehu rānei ki Irāki.
reference: It warns No one can guarantee that any course of action in Iraq at this point will stop sectarian warfare, growing violence, or a slide toward chaos.

source: Ka tīmata te Rīpoata ki te inoi mō tētahi tautohetohe tūmatawhānui me te whakaritenga o tētahi whakaaetanga ki Ngā Whenua Tōpū o Amerika mō te kaupapa here ki te Waenga o te Rāwhiti.
reference: The Report opens with plea for open debate and the formation of a consensus in the United States about the policy towards the Middle East.

source: E aro nui ana te rīpoata ki ngā āhuatanga katoa kei te kaupapa here mō te kāhui whakahaere ki a Irāki, ā, e whakatenatena hoki i tētahi tirohanga hou.
reference: The Report is highly critical of almost every aspect of the present policy of the Executive towards Iraq and it urges an immediate change of direction.

source: Ko te tuatahi o ngā kupu taunaki e 78 kia puta he kaupapa hou i mua i te paunga o tēnei tau e tau ai ngā roherohenga whenua o Īraki hei ārai atu i te hunga urutomo mai, me te whakatū mai anō i ngā hononga i waenganui i ngā hoa piripono.
reference: First among its 78 recommendations is that a new diplomatic initiative should be taken before the end of this year to secure Iraq’s borders against hostile interventions and to re-establish diplomatic relations with its neighbors.

source: I te pō ō nanahi nei whakatauria ai e te kainganga ture ō nāianei, te Māreikura hoki o Argentina, a First Lady Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner ōna tūmanakotanga mō te perehitini ki La Plata, he taone 50 kiromita (31 maero) nei te tawhiti atu i Buenos Aires.
reference: Current senator and Argentine First Lady Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner announced her presidential candidacy yesterday evening in La Plata, a city 50 kilometers (31 miles) away from Buenos Aires.

source: I whakapuaki a Mrs. Kirchner i tana takune ki te tono i te tūranga perehitene ki te Argentine Theatre, arā ko te wāhi i whakamahia e ia hei tīmata i tana tautohe i te tau 2005 mō te Senate hei mema o te apatono ā-takiwā o Buenos Aires.
reference: Mrs. Kirchner announced her intention to run for president at the Argentine Theatre, the same location she used to start her 2005 campaign for the Senate as member of the Buenos Aires province delegation.

source: I whakakāngia te tautohe e te whawhai mō te utu ki te whakamāmā me te hanganga anō whai muri i Hurricane Katrina; arā he whakatoi te tapa a ētahi ringa poto i tērā hei "Bush's New Orleans Deal."
