reference: Even traditionally, though, not all Sámi have been involved in big scale reindeer husbandry, but lived from fishing, hunting and similar, having reindeer mostly as draft animals.

source: I ēnei rā nei he nui ngā Sāmi e mahi ana i ngā mahi o ēnei wā nei. Ka nui te moni e ahu mai ana i ngā mahi tūruhi i Sāpmi, te wāhi o Sāmi.
reference: Today many Sámi work in modern trades. Tourism is an important income in Sápmi, the Sámi area.

source: He ahakoa e horapa ana te whakamahinga, inarā i waenganui i te hunga ehara i te Romani, e whai whakaarotia te kupu "Gypsy" hei kupu kino i te nuinga o te wā nā tana hononga ki te whakaaro horapa kino me ngā whakaaro hē mō ngā tāngata Romani.
reference: Though it is widely used, especially among non-Romani, the word "Gypsy" is often considered offensive because of its associations with negative stereotypes and inaccurate perceptions of Romani people.

source: Mehemea ka pā tētahi tohu hāereere ki te whenua e torohia ana e koe, ka pāngia pea tō inihua hauora hāereere, tō inihua whakakore haerenga rānei.
reference: If the country you will be visiting becomes subject to a travel advisory, your travel health insurance or your trip cancellation insurance may be affected.

source: Ka hiahia hoki pea koe ki te aro atu ki ngā tohutohu o ngā kāwanatanga atu i tō ake, engari kei te hoahoatia ō rātou tohutohu mō te hunga nō tō rātou whenua ake.
reference: You may also wish to consult the advice of governments other than your own, but their advice is designed for their citizens.

source: Hei tētahi tauira, ka aro atu pea ngā kainoho nō Amerika ki te Rāwhiti Waenga i ngā āhuatanga rerekē i te hunga nō Ūropi, i ngā Arapa rānei.
reference: As one example, American citizens in the Middle East might face different situations from Europeans or Arabs.

source: Ko ngā kaitohutohu he kōrero whakarāpopoto noa mō ngā āhuatanga tōrangapū o te whenua kotahi.
reference: Advisories are merely a brief summary of the political situation in one country.

source: Ko ngā whakaaro he wairua whakahāwea, he whakaaro whānui rawa, kāore i te hōhonu, kāore i te whāiti te titiro.
reference: The views presented are often cursory, general and oversimplified compared to the more detailed information available elsewhere.

source: Ko te huarere kino ko te rerenga noa mō tētahi āhuatanga huarere mōrearea ka whakaritea pea e ia te kino, te whakararu papori nui, te matenga rānei o te tangata.
reference: Severe weather is the generic term for any dangerous weather phenomenon with the potential to cause damage, serious social disruption, or loss of human life.

source: Ka taea te huarere kino te puta mai ki ngā wāhi katoa o te ao, ā, he maha ngā momo rerekē, ka hāngai pea ki te takotoranga papa, te takoto whenua, me ngā āhuatanga kōhauhau.
reference: Severe weather can occur anywhere in the world, and there are different types of it, which can depend on geography, topography, and atmospheric conditions.

source: Ko ngā hau kaha, ko te hukawaitara, ko te ua nui rawa, ko ngā ahi tarakaka ētahi āhua, ētahi pānga o te huarere kino, pērā anō hoki i ngā rautupu, i ngā āwhiowhio, i ngā puahiritanga, i ngā haumātakataka anō hoki.
reference: High winds, hail, excessive precipitation, and wildfires are forms and effects of severe weather, as are thunderstorms, tornadoes, waterspouts, and cyclones.

source: Ko ngā āhuatanga huarere kino ā-rohe, ā-kaupeka hoki ko ngā huka kairākau, ko ngā ua puahiri, ko ngā paroro tio me ngā paroro kōnehu.
reference: Regional and seasonal severe weather phenomena include blizzards, snowstorms, ice storms, and dust storms.

source: E tīno akiaki ana i ngā tūruhi kia matāra ai rātou i ngā tūraru marangai kei whakararu i tō rātou wāhi i roto anō hoki i te hanga nei ki te whakararu i te haerenga ahakoa te aha.
reference: Travellers are strongly advised to be aware of any risk of severe weather affecting their area as they may affect any travel plans.

source: Me whai whakangungu ngaio tētahi e whakamahere ana i te toronga atu ki tētahi whenua e whai whakaarohia ana he wāhi pakanga.
reference: Anyone planning a visit to a country that could be considered a war zone should get professional training.

source: Mā te rapu i te ipurangi mo te "kōhi taiao kino" e kite ai i te wahi noho o tētahi kamupene ā-rohe.
reference: A search of the Internet for 'Hostile environment course' will probably provide the address of a local company.

source: I te nuinga o te wā ka aro tētahi akomanga ki te katoa o ngā take e kōrerotia ana ki konei me te taipitopito nui ake, i te nuinga o te wā ki te wheako ā-ringa.
reference: A course will normally cover all the issues discussed here in far greater detail, usually with practical experience.

source: I te nuinga o te wā i waenga i te 2-5 ngā rā te roa o tētahi wānanga, ā, he whakatau kei roto, he nui te āwhina tuatahi, ā, i ētahi wā he whakangungu rākau.
reference: A course will normally be from 2-5 days and will involve role play, a lot of first aid and sometimes weapons training.

source: He tini ngā pukapuka me ngā makahīni e kōrero ana mō te mōrehutanga ki te wao, heoi ehara i te mea he nui ngā putanga e pā ana ki ngā wāhi pakanga.
reference: Books and magazines dealing with wilderness survival are common, but publications dealing with war zones are few.

source: Ko ngā hunga haere e whakamahere ana i te poka panoni ira kei tāwāhi me whakarite e kawe ana rātou i ngā puka whai mana mō te hokinga mai.
reference: Voyagers planning sex reassignment surgery abroad must ensure they're carrying valid documents for the return trip.

source: Ko te hiahia o te kāwanatanga ki te tuku uruuruwhenua ki te kore e puakina te ira (X), ki ngā puka kua whakahoungia kia ōrite ki tētahi ingoa e hiahiatia ana, ira e hiahiatia ana rāne, ka rerekē tētahi i tētahi.
reference: The willingness of governments to issue passports with gender not stated (X) or documents updated to match a desired name and gender varies.

source: Ko te hiahia a te kāwana manene ki whakahōnore i ēnei pukapuka, he rerekē noa atu hoki.
reference: Willingness of foreign governments to honour these documents is just as widely variable.

source: Kua manioro ake ngā whakatewhatewhanga i ngā tūngā a ngā kaiwhakahaumaru i te wā whai muri i te Hepetema 11, 2001.
reference: Searches at security checkpoints have also become far more intrusive in the post-September 11, 2001 era.

source: Ko te hunga irawhiti i mua i te hāparapara me kaua e pōhēhē ka puta i ngā matawai me te ū tonu o te noho matatapu me te mana.
reference: Pre-operative transgender people should not expect to pass through the scanners with their privacy and dignity intact.

source: Ko ngā ia kauere te hokinga mai o ngā ngaru e tūātea ana i te takutai moana, i te nuinga o te wā kei ngā ākau, kei ngā mea pērā rānei.
reference: Rip currents are the returning flow from waves breaking off the beach, often at a reef or similar.

source: I te takotoranga o te papa ki raro i te wai e pīhangaiti ana te pari auraki ki ngā wāhanga hohonu, ā, kei hua ake hoki tētahi pari tere ki te wai hōhonu ki reira.
reference: Due to the underwater topology the return flow is concentrated at a few deeper sections, and a fast current to deep water may form there.

source: Ko te nuinga o ngā mate ka ara ake i te ngenge i te wā e āki ana kia kaukau atu ki te au, i te mea akuanei pea he unuhanga taniwha tēnā.
reference: Most deaths happen as result of fatigue trying to swim back against the current, which may be impossible.

source: Ka puta tonu atu koe i te ia, ko te kaukau kia hoki atu kāore e uaua ake i te kaukau i te nuinga o te wā.
reference: As soon as you get out of the current, swimming back is no more difficult than normally.

source: Ngana ki te aro atu ki tētahi wāhi kāore koe e mau anō, i runga rānei i ō pukenga, i tō kitea atu rānei, me tatari pea koe kia whakarauoratia.
reference: Try aiming somewhere where you are not caught again or, depending on your skills and on whether you have been noticed, you might want to wait for rescue.

source: Ko te mauri rere o te kuhu anō ka tere ake te pā mai i tā te mauri rere ahurea (he iti iho te wā hanimuni), ka roa ake, ā, ka kino ake hoki pea.
reference: Re-entry shock comes on sooner than culture shock (there's less of a honeymoon phase), lasts longer, and can be more severe.

source: Ko te hunga hāereere he māmā te urutau ki te ahurea hou, i ētahi wā he uaua tā rātou urutau anō ki tō rātou ahurea taketake.
reference: Travellers who had an easy time adjusting to the new culture sometimes have a particularly hard time readjusting to their native culture.

source: Ka hoki mai ana koe ki te wā kāinga, kua whai ahurea hou koe, ā, kua ngaro i a koe ētahi o ngā tikanga o te kāinga.
reference: When returning home after living abroad, you've adapted to the new culture and lost some of your habits from your home culture.

source: I tō haerenga tuatahitanga ki tāwāhi, i te mānawanawa, i te āroharoha ngā tangata, i runga i te mōhio me urutau ngā tūruhi ki tētahi whenua hou.
reference: When you went abroad at first, people were probably patient and understanding, knowing that travellers in a new country need to adapt.

source: E kore pea ngā tāngata e whai whakaaro ake ko te manawa rau, ko te māramatanga he mea waiwai hoki mō te hunga hāereere e hoki ana ki te kāinga.
reference: People may not anticipate that patience and understanding are also necessary for travellers returning home.

source: Ko te tangi oro me te whakaaturanga tīramarama tētahi o ngā mea hirahira katoa mō ngā tamariki.
reference: The pyramid sound and light show is one of the most interesting things in the area for kids.

source: Ka kitea ngā koeko ki te pōuri, ā, ka kitea i roto i te ngū i mua i te tīmatanga o te whakaaturanga.
reference: You can see the pyramids in the dark and you can see them in silence before the show begins.

source: I te nuinga o te wā ka rongo i te oro o ngā tūruhi me ngā kaihoko. Ko ngā paki e pā ana ki te oro me te rama he pēnei i tētahi puka paki.
reference: Usually you always here the sound of tourists and vendors. The story of the sound and light is just like a story book.

source: Ko te Sphnix ka noho hei tuarongo me te kaikōrero o tētahi paki tino roa.
reference: The Sphinx is set as the backdrop and the narrator of a long story.

source: Kei te whakaaturia ngā mata ki ngā koeko, ā, kei te tūramatia ngā tini koeko.
reference: The scenes are displayed on the pyramids and the different pyramids are lit up.

source: Ko ngā Moutere o Shetland ki te Tonga, he moutere i kita i te tau 1819, e taunahatia ana e te nui ngā iwi o te ao, e 16 ngā moutere e rikarika ana.
reference: South Shetland Islands, discovered in 1819, are claimed by several nations and have the most bases, with sixteen active in 2020.

source: Kei te noho te huinga motu te 120 km ki te raki o te Raenga Kūiti. Ko te mea nui rawa ko King George Island me tōna kāinga o Villa Las Estrellas.
reference: The archipelago lies 120 km north of the Peninsula. The largest is King George Island with the settlement of Villa Las Estrellas.

source: Ko ētahi atu ko Livingston Island, me Deception kei reira tētahi whanga māori mīharo ki roto i te tawhā waipuke o tētahi rangitoto e oi tonu ana.
reference: Others include Livingston Island, and Deception where the flooded caldera of a still-active volcano provides a spectacular natural harbour.

source: Ko Ellsworth Land te takiwā ki te tonga o te raenga kūiti, e paengia ana e te Bellingshausen Sea.
reference: Ellsworth Land is the region south of the Peninsula, bounded by the Bellingshausen Sea.

source: Ko ngā pae maunga o konei ka whai wāhi atu ki te mānia, katahi ka rewa ake anō ki te hanga i te 360 km hononga o ngā Māunga Ellsworth, e weheruangia ana e te awa kōpaka o Minnesota.
reference: The mountains of the Peninsula here merge into the plateau, then re-emerge to form the 360 km chain of the Ellsworth Mountains, bisected by the Minnesota Glacier.

source: Kei te wāhanga raki, kei te Sentinel Range rānei ngā maunga teitei o Te Kōpakatanga ki te Tonga, arā ko te Vinson Massif, ko tōna tihi nui ko Mount Vinson ki te 4892 m.
reference: The northern part or Sentinel Range has Antarctica's highest mountains, the Vinson Massif, peaking at 4892 m Mount Vinson.

source: Ki ngā wāhi mohoao, karekau te pānga waea pūkoro, ko tētahi waea āmiorangi pea tō kōwhiringa kotahi.
reference: In remote locations, without cell phone coverage, a satellite phone may be your only option.

source: Ehara te waea āmiorangi i te tino whakakapinga mō tētahi waea pūkoro, nā te mea me noho ki waho i te whare me te tirohanga wātea atu ki te āmiorangi kia pato i tētahi waeatanga atu.
reference: A satellite phone is not generally a replacement for a mobile phone, as you have to be outdoors with clear line of sight to the satellite to make a phone call.

source: Kei te whakamahia auautia te ratonga e te kaipuke, tae atu hoki ki ngā waka rēhia, waihoki ko ngā haerenga he hiahia ō rātou mō te raraunga me te reo mamao.
reference: The service is frequently used by shipping, including pleasure craft, as well as expeditions who have remote data and voice needs.

source: Me āhei tō kaituku ratonga waea ā-rohe te hoatu i ētahi atu mōhiohio mō te tūhono ki tēnei ratonga.
reference: Your local telephone service provider should be able to give more information about connecting to this service.

source: Ko tētahi kōwhiringa e piki haere ana te rorotu mō rātou e whakamahere ana i tētahi tau-waenga ko te hāereere me te ako.
reference: An increasingly more popular option for those planning a gap-year is to travel and learn.

source: He pai rawa atu tēnei ki ngā ika ā-whiro, e tukuna ana rātou kia whakatā mō te kotahi tau i runga i te mōhio ka kore e pā mai he raru ki ā rātou e whai tohu ana.
reference: This is especially popular with school leavers, allowing them to take a year out before university, without compromising their education.

source: He nui ngā wā, ko te uru atu ki tētahi akoranga tau-waenga ki tāwāhi ka mātua whakapakari ake i te tupono ka neke atu koe ki te mātauranga teitei ake ki tō whenua ūkaipō.
reference: In many cases, enrolling on a gap-year course abroad can actually improve your chances of moving into higher education back in your home country.

source: I te nuinga o te wā he utu kura kia whakauru ki ēnei kaupapa ako.
reference: Typically there will be a tuition fee to enroll in these educational programs.

source: He wāhi pai a Whinarana mō te taunga waka. Kei "Te Whenua o ngā rotomano", ngā motu manomano hoki, ki ngā roto me ngā huinga motu i te takutai.
reference: Finland is a great boating destination. The "Land of a thousand lakes" has thousands of islands too, in the lakes and in the coastal archipelagos.

source: Mō te āhua ki ngā moutere me ngā roto, hei aha noa iho te hērā.
reference: In the archipelagos and lakes you do not necessarily need a yacht.

source: Ahakoa ka rawaka pū te rahi o ngā huinga motu taha ki te whenua, me ngā roto rawhi rawa mō tētahi iata, mā ngā poti paku iho, mā te kōreti rānei ka puta he wheako rerekē.
reference: Although the coastal archipelagos and the biggest lakes are indeed big enough for any yacht, smaller boats or even a kayak offer a different experience.

source: He mahi pārekareka te eke kaipuke ki roto o Finland, ko te kaipuka kotahi ki 7 tāngata, 8 tāngata rānei.
reference: Boating is a national pastime in Finland, with a boat to every seven or eight people.

source: I rite nei tēnei ki Nōwei, ki Huitene, ki Aotearoa hoki, engari tonu hoki he tino ahurei (hei tauira, ki te whenua Hōrana he tahi ki te whā tekau te nama).
reference: This is matched by Norway, Sweden and New Zealand, but otherwise quite unique (e.g. in the Netherlands the figure is one to forty).

source: Kei te nuinga o ngā Baltic Cruises tētahi noho āhua roa ki St. Petersburg, ki Rūhia.
reference: Most of the distinct Baltic Cruises feature an extended stay in St. Petersburg, Russia.

source: Ko te tikanga ka āhei mai ki tēnei whenua rirerire mō ngā rā e rua, ka hoki mai ki te kaipuke moe ai i ngā pō.
reference: This means you can visit the historic city for a couple of full days while returning and sleeping on the ship at night.

source: Mēnā koe ka pae ki uta mā runga kaipike, kaore noa he take o te pane uruwhenua (atu i te tau 2009).
reference: If you only go ashore using shipboard excursions you will not need a separate visa (as of 2009).

source: Kei ngā puka o ētahi rerenga moana ko Berlin ki Tiamani. Ka kitea koe ki te mahere ki runga kāore i te paku tata a Berlin ki te moana, ā, ko te toronga ki te tāone nui kāore i roto i te utu o te rerenga.
reference: Some cruises feature Berlin, Germany in the brochures. As you can see from the map above Berlin is no where near the sea and a visit to the city is not included in the price of the cruise.

source: Ka wehi te tangata ahakoa te pakeke ahakoa hoki nō hea ki te haerere mā te rererangi, ko te nuinga o te wā me kaore anō ia i rere, kua kino rānei tētahi wheakoranga.
reference: Travelling by plane can be a scary experience for people of all ages and backgrounds, particularly if they've not flown before or have experienced a traumatic event.

source: Kāore he take kia whakamā: ehara i te rerekē i ngā mataku me ngā hae whaiaro ki ētahi atu mea kei te tokomaha.
reference: It is not something to be ashamed of: it is no different from the personal fears and dislikes of other things that very many people have.

source: Ki ētahi, mā te mārama ki ētahi āhuatanga o te mahi a ngā waka rererangi, me ngā mahi i te wā o te rerenga, he mea tautoko pea hei patu i te mataku e noho ana ki ngā āhuatanga kāore i te mōhiotia, o te noho kore mana whakahaere rānei.
reference: For some, understanding something about how aircraft work and what happens during a flight may help to overcome a fear which is based on the unknown or on not being in control.

source: E utua paitia ana ngā kamupene koria mō te kawe tere i ngā mea. He nui ngā wā, he hirahira te wā mō te taha ki ngā pukapuka kamupene, ki ngā taputapu, ki ngā wāhanga mō tētahi whakatikanga whitawhita.
reference: Courier companies are well paid for delivering things quickly. Frequently, time is very important with business documents, merchandise or spare parts for an urgent repair.

source: I ētahi ara, he rererangi tā tēnā kamupene, tā tēnā kamupene, engari mō ētahi atu rā, i pā mai ētahi raruraru.
reference: On some routes, the larger companies have their own planes, but for other routes and smaller firms there was a problem.

source: Mēnā rātou i tono i ngā mea mā runga, i ētahi ara ka ruarua pea ngā rā e uru mai ai i te wāhi tangotango me te whakatō.
reference: If they sent things by air freight, on some routes it may have taken days to get through unloading and customs.

source: E tere ake ai, me tuku ki te wāhi tuku pēke. Ko ngā ture a te taunga rererangi, ka kore e tukuna ngā pēke ki te kore tētahi tangata e rere, ko koe tēnā.
reference: The only way to get it through faster was to send it as checked luggage. Airline regulations will not allow them to send luggage without a passenger, which is where you come in.

source: Ko te huarahi mārakerake te kite mō te rere i ngā tūru rangatira i ngā tūru mahi rānei he hoatu i tētahi moni mātotoru ki te hunga whairawa ( ka pai ake mēnā ka taea e koe tō kamupene te mahi māu).
reference: The obvious way of flying in first or business class is to fork out a thick wad of money for the privilege (or, better yet, get your company to do it for you).

source: Heoi anō, ehara i te mea he iti te utu o tēnei: hei tohu āwhiwhi, ka utu pea koe i te whā whakareatanga o te utu iti te utu noa mō te pakihi, me te tekau mā tahi whakareanga mō te taumata tuatahi!
reference: However, this does not come cheap: as rough rules of thumb, you can expect to pay up to four times the normal economy fare for business, and eleven times for first class!

source: I te nuinga o te wā, kāore he paku take o te rapu noa i ngā hekenga utu mō ngā tūru pakihi, karaehe-tuatahi rānei ki ngā rerenga tōtika i A ki B.
reference: Generally speaking, there is no point in even looking for discounts for business or first-class seats on direct flights from A to B.

source: Kei te mōhio pai ngā umanga waka rererangi he rōpū matua o te hunga rere e pai ana ki te utu i te utu nui rawa kia tae tere atu, āhuru atu ki tētahi wāhi, ā, ka pērā te whakarite utu.
reference: Airlines know well that there is a certain core group of flyers who are willing to pay top dollar for the privilege of getting somewhere fast and in comfort, and charge accordingly.

source: Ko te tāone matua o Moldova ko Chisinau. Ko tō rātou reo ko te reo Romānia engari kei te kōrerotia whānuitia te reo Rūhia.
reference: The capital of Moldova is Chişinău. The local language is Romanian, but Russian is widely used.

source: Ko Moldova tētahi whenua tini mātāwaka kua pāngia e te raru mātāwaka.
reference: Moldova is a multi-ethnic republic that has suffered from ethnic conflict.

source: I te tau 1994, i ara ake tēnei pakanga ki te hanganga o te Transnistria Republic ki te rāwhiti o Moldova nā rātou anō i whakapuaki, kei a rātou tō rātou ake kāwanatanga, pūnaha moni anō hoki, engari kāore i te whakamanatia e tētahi atu whenua he mema nō te Kotahitanga o Ngā Iwi o te Ao.
reference: In 1994, this conflict led to the creation of the self-proclaimed Transnistria Republic in eastern Moldova, which has its own government and currency but is not recognised by any UN member country.

source: Kua whakatūria anō ngā hononga ohaoha i waenganui i ēnei wāhanga e rua o Moldova ahakoa te hinganga ki ngā whakaritenga tōrangapū.
reference: Economic links have been re-established between these two parts of Moldova despite the failure in political negotiations.

source: Ko te hāhi matua ki Moldova ko te Karaitiana Tūturu.
reference: The major religion in Moldova is Orthodox Christian.

source: Ko Izmir te tāone nui he tuatoru tōna rahi ki Turkey me te taupori o te tata ki te 3.7 miriona, te xxx tuarua te rahi whai muri i Istanbul, me tētahi poka pū ikiiki tino pai.
reference: İzmir is the third largest city in Turkey with a population of around 3.7 million, the second biggest port after Istanbul, and a very good transport hub.

source: I tētahi wā te taone ukiuki nei o Smyrna, i tēnei wā he hou, he whanaketanga me tētahi pokapū e pōkea ana ki te mahi, ā, kei te takiwa o tētahi koko nui me ngā māunga e karapoti nei.
reference: Once the ancient city of Smyrna, it is now a modern, developed, and busy commercial center, set around a huge bay and surrounded by mountains.

source: Ko ngā huarahi whānui, ko ngā whare he karāhe te pakitara o mua, me ngā pokapū hokohoko hou e kōtingotingo ana i waenga i ngā tuanui papauku-whero, te mākete nō te rautau 18, me ngā mosque me ngā whare karakia tawhito, ahakoa kei te tāone nui te wairua ka nui ake te Mediterranean Europe i te Tākei o mua.
reference: The broad boulevards, glass-fronted buildings and modern shopping centers are dotted with traditional red-tiled roofs, the 18th century market, and old mosques and churches, although the city has an atmosphere more of Mediterranean Europe than traditional Turkey.

source: Ko tā te kāinga o Haldarsvik he tirohanga ki te motu tata o Esturoy, ā, he karakia tapawaru rerekē ki reira.
reference: The village of Haldarsvík offer views of the nearby island Eysturoy and has an unusual octagonal church.

source: I roto i te whare karakia, he whakapakoko kukupa māpere nei kei runga i ētahi urupā.
reference: In the churchyard, there are interesting marble sculptures of doves over some tombs.

source: Hāwhe haora tō karore hāere i tēnei kāinga, he mea whakamiharo.
reference: It's worth half an hour to stroll about the intriguing village.

source: Ki te raki, ā, he māmā te tae atu ki reira, ko te tāone whakaingoingo, manawarū o Sintra, ā, i rongonui ai ki te hunga rāwaho i muri i te kōrero kauanuanu mō āna ātanga nā Lord Byron i tuhi.
reference: To the north and within easy reach is the romantic and fascinating town of Sintra and which was made famous to foreigners after a glowing account of its splendours recorded by Lord Byron.

source: He rite tonu te haere a te Pahi Scotturb 403 ki Sintra, me te tau hoki ki Cabo da Roca.
reference: Scotturb Bus 403 travels regularly to Sintra, stopping at Cabo da Roca.

source: Ki te raki hoki me toro i te Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima (Shrine) nui whakaharahara, he wāhi o ngā mariko Marion rongonui puta noa i te ao.
reference: Also to the north visit the great Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima (Shrine), a place of worldwide famous Marian apparitions.

source: Kia maumahara kei te mātua toro atu koe ki tētahi wāhi nehu nui rawa, tae atu hoki ki tētahi wāhi e whai ana i te māramatanga e kore e taea te tatau ki tētahi wāhanga nui o te taupori o te ao.
reference: Please remember that you are essentially visiting a mass grave site, as well as a site that has an almost incalculable meaning to a significant portion of the world's population.

source: He tini tonu ngā tāne me ngā wāhine i mōrehutia ki konei, ā, he tini ake i ngaro i a rātou ō rātou makau nā te patunga, nā te mahinga kia mate rānei, ngā Hūrae me ngā Hūrae kore hoki.
reference: There are still many men and women alive who survived their time here, and many more who had loved ones who were murdered or worked to death there, Jews and non-Jews alike.

source: Tēnā koa whai whakaaro mai ki te mana, te ihi, te wehi o tēnei wāhi. Kaua e pukuhohe mō ngā mahi whakapoauau a ngā Nazi.
reference: Please treat the site with all of the dignity, solemnity and respect it deserves. Do not make jokes about the Holocaust or Nazis.

source: Kauka te wāhi e whakaanuanu mā te māka mā te raku i te karawhiti ki ngā hanganga.
reference: Do not deface the site by marking or scratching graffiti into structures.

source: Ko ngā reo ōkawa o Barcelona ko Catalan me Pāniora. Tata ki te haurua ka pai ake ki te kōrero Catalan, ka māramatia e te tino nuinga, ā, tata ki te katoa e mōhio ana ki te reo Pāniora.
reference: Barcelona's official languages are Catalan and Spanish. About a half prefer to speak Catalan, a vast majority understands it, and virtually everyone knows Spanish.

source: Heoi anō, kei te tohua te nuinga o ngā tohu ki te reo Catalan anake nā te mea kua whakaūngia e te ture koirā te reo ōkawa tuatahi.
reference: However, most signs are indicated only in Catalan because it is established by law as the first official language.

source: Heoi, E māhorahora ana te kōrerotia o te reo Pāniora ki ngā wāhi tūmatanui me ērā momo wāhi.
reference: Yet, Spanish is also widely used in public transport and other facilities.

source: Ko ngā pānui auau i te Metro e mahi noa ana ki Catalan, heoi ko ngā putanga kōrero raruraru kore perēne nā te pūnaha mīhini auau i roto i ngā tini reo pēnei i te Paniora, te Ingarihi, te Wīwi, te Araby me te Hapanihi.
reference: Regular announcements in the Metro are made only in Catalan, but unplanned disruptions are announced by an automated system in a wide variety of languages including Spanish, English, French, Arabic and Japanese.

source: E mōhiotia ana te iwi Parisia he iwi whakahīhī, he kuare, he whakamenemene.
reference: Parisians have a reputation for being egocentric, rude and arrogant.

source: Ahakoa he nui ngā wā he whakaaro horapa hē tēnei, ko te ara pai rawa kia pai te haere ki Parī kia pai rawa tō whanonga, me pērā i tētahi tangata he "bien eleve" (kua pai te whakatipu). Mā korā e māmā ake ai te neke haere.
reference: While this is often only an inaccurate stereotype, the best way to get along in Paris still is to be on your best behavior, acting like someone who is "bien élevé" (well brought up). It will make getting about considerably easier.

source: Ko te āhua māro o te hunga nō Parī ka tere ngaro atu mēnā ka whakaatu koe i ētahi atawhaitanga māmā.
reference: Parisians' abrupt exteriors will rapidly evaporate if you display some basic courtesies.

source: He nui te ngahere ki te Plitvice Lakes national park, ko te nuinga he rākau beech, he spruce, he fir, ā, he huinga o ngā otaota Alpine, Mediterranean hoki ki reira.
reference: The Plitvice Lakes national park is heavily forested, mainly with beech, spruce, and fir trees, and features a mixture of Alpine and Mediterranean vegetation.

source: He tino whānui te rerenga o ngā hapori tipu, i runga i ōna āhuarangi-whāiti, ōna one rerekē, me ngā taumata teitei rerekē.
reference: It has a notably wide variety of plant communities, due to its range of microclimates, differing soils and varying levels of altitude.

source: He kāinga hoki tēnei rohe ki te tino whānuitanga rawa o te rerenga o ngā momo kararehe, manu hoki.
reference: The area is also home to an extremely wide variety of animal and bird species.

source: Ko ngā kararehe ongeonge pērā i te pia parāone o Ūropi, i te wuruhi, i te īkara, i te koukou, i te riniki, i te ngeru mohoao, i te capercaillie ka kitea ki reira, me ngā tini momo anō hoki.
reference: Rare fauna such as the European brown bear, wolf, eagle, owl, lynx, wild cat and capercaillie can be found there, along with many more common species

source: I te wā e toro ana ki ngā whare monaki, e hiahiatia ana te wahine ki te mau panekoti e uwhi ana i ngā turi, ā, kei te uwhia hoki ō rātou pakihiwi.
reference: While visiting the monasteries, women are required to wear skirts covering the knees and have their shoulders covered, too.

source: Ko te nuinga o ngā whare karakia ka tuku i ngā takai mā ngā wāhine ka taetae atu me te kore i whakarite, engari ki te kawe atu koe i tō ake takai, inarā ko tētahi he kitakita ngā tae ka menemene mai te manake, te none rānei ki te tomokanga.
reference: Most of the monasteries do provide wraps for women who come unprepared, but if you bring your own, especially one with bright colors, you'll get a smile from the monk or nun at the entrance.

source: Ko taua āhua anō rā, me mau tarau ngā tāne kia kore ai e kitea ngā pona.
reference: Along the same line, men are required to wear trousers covering the knees.

source: Ka taea hoki tēnei te mino mai i ngā rawa ki te tomokanga engari kāore ērā kākahu i te horoia whai muri i ia kaimahi nō reira e kore pea koe e pai ki te mau i ēnei panekoti. E pai ana te rahi kotahi ki ngā tāne katoa!
reference: This too can be borrowed from the stock at the entrance but that clothing isn't washed after every user so you may not feel comfortable wearing these skirts. One size fits all for men!

source: Ko ngā kai Majorcan, e rite ana ki ētahi wāhi ki Metatareina, he paraoa te kai matua, he huawhenua, he mīti (kōia tonu ko te poaka), me te whakamahinga o te hinu oriwa hoki.
reference: Majorcan cuisine, like that of similar zones in the Mediterranean, is based on bread, vegetables and meat (specially pork), and uses olive oil throughout.

source: Ko tētahi tina rorotu, inarā i te wā o te raumati, ko te Pa amb Oli: Te parāoa ki te hinu ōriwa, te tomato, me ngā kīnaki e wātea ana pērā i te tīhi, te ika, aha atu.
reference: A simple popular dinner, especially during the summer, is the Pa amb Oli: Bread with olive oil, tomato, and any available condiments such as cheese, tunafish, etc.

source: Ko ngā kupu ingoa katoa, tae atu ki te kupu Sie mōu, ka tīmata ki te pūmatua, ahakoa kei waenganui i te rerenga.
reference: All nouns, alongside the word Sie for you, always begin with a capital letter, even in the middle of a sentence.

source: He ara hira tēnei hei whakawehe i waenga i ētahi kupu mahi me ngā aronga.
reference: This is an important way to distinguish between some verbs and objects.

source: Ka taea hoki te kī kei te whakamāmā ia i te pānui, ahakoa kei te whakatāwhiwhitia te tuhituhi e te hiahia kia whai mōhio mēnā rānei kei te whakamahia tētahi kupu mahi, kupu whakaahua rānei ki tētahi āhua whakakupuingoa.
reference: It also arguably makes reading easier, though writing is somewhat complicated by the need to find out whether a verb or adjective is used in a substantivized form.

source: He māmā noa te whakahua i te reo Itāria nō te mea e ōrite ana te whakahua o te nuinga o ngā kupu ki te takotoranga tuhi.
reference: Pronunciation is relatively easy in Italian since most words are pronounced exactly how they are written

source: Ko ngā reta matua me mataara ko te c me te g, i te mea e rerekē ai te whakahuatanga e ai ki te oropuare e whai ake ana.
reference: The main letters to watch out for are c and g, since their pronunciation varies based on the following vowel.

source: Me te mea hoki, me rerekē te whakahuatanga o te r hei me te rr: ko te caro he tia, ā, ko te carro he hāriata.
reference: Also, make sure to pronounce r and rr differently: caro means dear, whereas carro means chariot.

source: Kei te reo Pāhia tētahi wete reo āhua māmā, āhua riterite.
reference: Persian has a relatively easy and mostly regular grammar.

source: Nā reira, mā te pānui i ēnei pukapuka wetereo e mārama ake ai koe ki ngā tikanga wetereo ki Persia, me te whakatakotoranga o ngā rerenga kōrero.
reference: Therefore, reading this grammar primer would help you learn much about Persian grammar and understand phrases better.

source: E pūrangiaho te kitea, mēnā ka mōhio koe ki tētahi reo mate kanehe,, ka māmā ake tāu ako i te reo Potukara.
reference: Needless to say, if you know a Romance language, it will be easier for you to learn Portuguese.

source: Heoi anō, ko te hunga e mōhio ana ki te paku reo Pāniora ka tere whakatau pea ka pērā te ōrite o te reo Potukara kia kaua e ako wehe i taua reo.
reference: However, people who know a little Spanish may hastily conclude that Portuguese is close enough that it need not be studied separately.

source: Ko te nuinga o ngā whare mātai ātea tawhito e korehāhā ana i tēnei rā, ā, ka noho tohu hei whare taonga, hei wāhi whakaako rānei.
reference: Pre-modern observatories are usually obsolete today, and remain as museums, or sites of education.

source: I te mea mea, kāore e pērā rawa ana te kino o te hauārangi kino i te wā i a rātou, ka kitea i ngā tāone kei taua takiwā me ngā kuratini, he māmā ake te toro atu i ngā mea i hangaia ō mohoa nei.
reference: As light pollution in their heyday was not the kind of problem it is today, they are usually located in cities or at campuses, easier to reach than those built in modern times.

source: Ko te nuinga o ngā karu whātata rangahau hou he hanganga rahi rawa ki ngā wāhi kōkēi me ngā āhuatanga kōhauhau pai.
reference: Most modern research telescopes are enormous facilities in remote areas with favorable atmospheric conditions.

source: Ko te hanami he tirotiro ki ngā pūāwai tiere, ā, kua pēnei te ahurea Hapanihi mai i te rautau tuawaru.
reference: Cherry blossom viewing, known as hanami, has been a part of Japanese culture since the 8th century.

source: I ahu mai te whakaaro nei i Haina ko reira whiriwhiri ai te putiputi paramu hei kōwhiringa pua.
reference: The concept came from China where plum blossoms were the flower of choice.

source: Ki Hapāni, ko te pua puaina tuatahi nā te emepara i whakatū mōna anake, me ōna apataki o te kāhui ariki me te kōti teitei.
reference: In Japan, the first cherry blossom parties were hosted by the emperor only for himself and other members of the aristocracy around the Imperial Court.

source: He pai ake te hanga o te rākau i roto i tōna ake taiao, nōreira me kauaka e kato i "tētahi" paku mea mai i tana wāhi.
reference: Plants look their best when in a natural environment, so resist the temptation to remove even "just one" specimen.

source: Ki te toro atu koe ki tētahi māra kua āta whakamaheretia, mā te kohi "pota" ka whakaputaina koe, karekau te kōrerorero.
reference: If visiting a formally arranged garden, collecting "specimens" is also going to get you ejected, without discussion.

source: I te nuinga o te wā he wāhi tīno haumrau a Singapore me te ngāwari hoki ki te hautū haere, e taea ana e koe te nuinga o ngā mea te hoko i muri i te taenga atu.
reference: Singapore is generally an extremely safe place to be and very easy to navigate, and you can buy almost anything after arriving.

source: Ēngari nā te noho ki ngā "takiwa tino pārū" ētahi tīkiri noa ki te raki o te weheruatanga o te ao ka pāngia koe e te wera (i ngā wā katoa) me te kaha o te rā (i te wā he rangi paki, he iti ake ngā wā).
reference: But being placed in the "high tropics" just a few degrees north of equator you will need to deal with both heat (always) and strong sun (when the sky is clear, more rarely).

source: Tērā hoki ētahi pahi e haere whakateraki ana ki Hebron, te wāhi urupā taketake o te pā whakawairua o Aperehama nō te Paipera, o Ihaka, o Hāpoka me ā rātou wāhine.
reference: There are also a few buses going north to Hebron, the traditional burial place of the Biblical patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and their wives.

source: Me whakatūturu ko te pahi e whakaaro ana koe kia eke ka haere ki Hebron, kaua ki te kāinga Hūrae tata o Kiryat Arba noa iho.
reference: Check that the bus you are thinking of taking goes into Hebron and not just to the nearby Jewish settlement of Kiryat Arba.

source: He pai ngā arawai o uta hei kaupapa ārahi i ō haerenga.
reference: Inland waterways can be a good theme to base a holiday around.

source: Hei tauira ko te toro atu ki ngā whare kīngi ki te Loire Valley, ki te Rhine valley, te eke rānei ki tētahi rerenga waka ki ngā tāone nui manawa reka ki te Danube, te eke waka rānei ki te Erie Canal.
reference: For example visiting castles in the Loire Valley, the Rhine valley or taking a cruise to interesting cites on the Danube or boating along the Erie Canal.

source: Ka tautuhia hoki ngā ara mō te hīkoi rongonui me ngā ara paihikara.
reference: They also define routes for popular hiking and cycling trails.

source: Ko te Kirihimete ko tētahi o ngā hararei whakahirahira rawa o te Karaitiana, ā, ka whakanuia hei rāwhānau o Ihu.
reference: Christmas is one of the most important holidays of Christianity, and is celebrated as the birthday of Jesus.

source: He nui tonu ngā tīkanga mō te hararei kua kawea hoki e ngā tāngata-kore-whakapono i ngā whenua Karaitiana me te hunga ehara i te Karaitiana huri noa i te ao.
reference: Many of the traditions surrounding the holiday have been adopted also by non-believers in Christian countries and non-Christians around the world.

source: Arā tētahi tikanga kia noho oho mō te roanga o te pō Aranga ki tētahi wāhi mārakerake hei kite i te aranga mai o te rā.
reference: There's a tradition to pass the Easter night awake at some exposed point to see the sunrise.

source: He whakamāramatanga ā-hāhi Karaitiana tonu mō tēnei tikanga, engari tonu he ritenga pea nō mua i te wā Karaitiana mō te Kōanga me te Matahua.
reference: There are of course Christian theological explanations for this tradition, but it may well be a pre-Christian Spring and Fertility ritual.

source: Ko ngā whare karakia tūturu ake o mua ka mau tonu ki te Easter Vigil i te pō o te Hatarei i te wā o te mutunga wiki Aranga, ā, ko ngā minenga ka puta ki te whakanuitanga i te wā pū tonu o te tūruawēpō kia whakanui i te aranga mai o Ihu.
reference: More traditional churches often hold an Easter Vigil on Saturday night during the Easter weekend, with the congregations often breaking into celebration at the stroke of midnight to celebrate Christ's resurrection.

source: Ko ngā kararehe katoa i tae tūturu mai ki ngā motu i tae mai ki konei nā te kaukau, te rere hau, te rere wai rānei.
reference: All animals that originally arrived in the islands came here either by swimming, flying or floating.

source: E ai ki te roanga mai i te paparahi kāore ngā whāngote i āhei te haere nō reira ko te giant tortoise te kararehe kai otaota matua ki ngā Galapagos.
reference: Due to the long distance from the continent mammals were unable to make the journey making the giant tortoise the primary grazing animal in the Galapagos.

source: Mai anō i te taenga mai o te tangata ki Galapagos, kua tomokia atu ngā tini kararehe pēnei i ngā koti, ngā hoiho, kau, kiore, ngeru me ngā kurī.
reference: Since the arrival of man to the Galapagos, many mammals have been introduced including goats, horses, cows, rats, cats and dogs.

source: Mēnā koe ka haere ki te Arctic, ki te Antarctic rānei i ngā wā o te takurua, ka kite koa i te pō poara, tōna tikanga hoki kāore te rā e awatea mai i te paerangi.
reference: If you visit the Arctic or Antarctic areas in the winter you will experience the polar night, which means that the sun doesn't rise above the horizon.

source: He hoatutanga pai tēnei kia kite ai i ngā Aurora Borealis, i te mea ka pōuri te rangi mō te roanga o te huringa o te karaka.
reference: This offers a good opportunity to see the Aurora borealis, as the sky will be dark more or less around the clock.

source: I te mea kua itiiti nei te rahinga tāngata, kāore he tino raruraru o te parakino-raiti, ā, ka manawareka anō hoki koe ki ngā whetū.
reference: As the areas are sparsely populated, and light pollution therefore often not a problem, you will also be able to enjoy the stars.

source: He nui ake te ara ā-kapa, te ōkawa hoki o te ahurea mahi o Hapani i tā te hunga Uru e waia ana.
reference: Japanese work culture is more hierarchical and formal that what Westerners may be used to.

source: He māori noa te mau hūtu mō te pakihi, ā, ka karangatia ngā kaimahi e rātou ake ki ō rātou ingoa whānau ki ō rātou taitara mahi hoki rānei.
reference: Suits are standard business attire, and coworkers call each other by their family names or by job titles.

source: He mea waiwai te reretahi ki te wāhi mahi, me te aro atu ki te mahi a te rōpū kaua ko te mihi ki ngā ekenga takitahi.
reference: Workplace harmony is crucial, emphasizing group effort rather than praising individual accomplishments.

source: He nui ngā wā me whai whakaaetanga ngā kaimahi i ō rātou rangatira mō ngā whakatau katoa, ā, e hiahiatia ana ka whai rātou i ngā tohutohu o ō rātou rangatira horekau te pātai atu.
reference: Workers must often get their superiors' approval for any decisions they make, and are expected to obey their superiors' instructions without question.
